review::.::holistic::cross_module_archit.summary: Shared modules perform startup work at import time, creating order-dependent coupling.

evidence: `examples/a2a_gateway/01_basic_gateway.py` executes `setup_logging()` at module scope, so importing the module mutates process logging configuration.
`tests/support/integration/config.py` creates `test_settings = TestSettings()` at module scope, binding environment/file-derived config during import rather than fixture/runtime boundaries.
detail.suggestion: Move import-time initialization behind explicit functions/fixtures: call logging setup only inside `main()`, and replace module-global `test_settings` with a fixture or cached accessor initialized on first use.

review::.::holistic::cross_module_archit.summary: Link/WebSocket contract mocking is duplicated across tests instead of centralized.

evidence: `tests/runtime/test_presence.py` and `tests/runtime/test_runtime.py` each build large custom `mock_link` fixtures with overlapping async method stubs and iterator behavior.
`tests/platform/test_link_contacts.py` separately patches `WebSocketClient` and defines another transport contract mock pattern.
The same transport/event boundary is recreated in multiple files, increasing blast radius when link API shape changes.
detail.suggestion: Create a shared test boundary layer (e.g., `tests/support/fakes_link.py` + shared fixtures in `conftest.py`) that provides canonical `ThenvoiLink`/WebSocket test doubles; keep per-test customization minimal via overrides.

review::.::holistic::cross_module_archit.summary: Runtime config and credential resolution are fragmented into parallel loaders.

evidence: `examples/common/bootstrap.py` implements env + agent config resolution (`load_platform_urls`, `load_runtime_config`).
`docker/shared/config_loader.py` separately parses YAML and injects credentials with its own merge semantics.
`examples/coding_agents/test_communication.py` adds another ad-hoc YAML loader (`load_agent_config`) that bypasses shared config-loading contracts.
detail.suggestion: Define one shared config-resolution module for scripts/examples (environment defaults, YAML parsing, credential injection, error policy) and have docker/example utilities depend on that boundary rather than re-implementing local loaders.

review::.::holistic::convention_outlier:.summary: Runner config loading convention diverged into duplicated implementations.

evidence: `docker/shared/config_loader.py` and `examples/claude_sdk_docker/runner.py:load_config()` both perform near-identical YAML parsing and ValueError wrapping.
The shared loader supports keyed sections (`agent_key`) and copies either section/global config into a new dict, while example runner hardcodes `agent` and mutates the loaded config in place.
`docker/claude_sdk/runner.py` uses the shared loader, but example runner keeps a local variant, creating two conventions for the same responsibility.
detail.suggestion: Consolidate runner config loading to one shared function (prefer `docker/shared/config_loader.py`), parameterize key selection for all runners, and remove the duplicate loader in `examples/claude_sdk_docker/runner.py`.

review::.::holistic::convention_outlier:.summary: Sibling integration packages use conflicting export conventions.

evidence: Some integration packages expose utility symbols via `__all__` and direct imports (langgraph, claude_sdk, a2a), while others publish empty surfaces (`__all__: list[str] = []`).
This is not just naming variation; it changes whether sibling packages behave like importable facades or documentation-only placeholders.
The pattern forces users to remember package-specific import rules instead of relying on one integration package convention.
detail.suggestion: Define and enforce a single integration package policy (facade exports or internal-only modules), document it, then align all `src/thenvoi/integrations/*/__init__.py` files to that policy.

review::.::holistic::error_consistency::.summary: Tool integrations mix exception bubbling and in-band string errors.

evidence: `graph_as_tool()` states and implements bubble-up behavior for subgraph errors (`await graph.ainvoke(...)` without catch).
`parlant/tools.py` catches broad `Exception` in `_run_tool()` and returns `ToolResult(data=format_tool_error(...))`, converting failures into normal tool return values.

`examples/langgraph/standalone_calculator.py` raises `ValueError` for invalid operations/divide-by-zero, which propagates through the LangGraph path but would be converted to string payloads in the Parlant path.
detail.suggestion: Adopt one cross-integration failure contract (typed exception mapping or structured error result object) and implement adapters that normalize errors before returning to framework-specific tool layers.

review::.::holistic::error_consistency::.summary: Runner prompt-file reads bypass the loaders’ normalized error strategy.

evidence: Both runners normalize config read/parse failures via ValueError in loader code paths.
Both runners read role prompt files with `prompt_file.read_text(...)` without local exception normalization.
A missing/unreadable prompt file in an existing path context can surface raw `OSError` while nearby config errors are wrapped uniformly, producing inconsistent boundary behavior.
detail.suggestion: Wrap prompt-file reads in the same normalization strategy used for config loading (e.g., catch `OSError` and raise a typed config/runtime setup error with path context).

review::.::holistic::abstraction_fitness.summary: Tool API abstraction is re-implemented per adapter, creating parallel contracts and drift risk.

evidence: `runtime/tools.py` already centralizes tool names/input models (`TOOL_MODELS`), but `pydantic_ai.py` hand-writes many wrapper funcs that differ mostly by tool name and arg dict.
`claude_sdk.py` maintains separate `_MCP_TOOL_SCHEMAS` with overlapping fields for the same tool set.
`crewai.py` introduces a third schema layer (`CrewAI*Input` + `CREWAI_TOOL_SCHEMAS`) for the same platform tools.
Tests in `test_pydantic_ai_adapter.py` assert tool-by-tool registration by name, reinforcing duplicated per-adapter surfaces instead of validating one shared source.
detail.suggestion: Introduce a single adapter-agnostic `ToolBindingRegistry` derived from `TOOL_MODELS` that can emit framework-specific bindings (PydanticAI function tools, CrewAI BaseTool classes, Claude MCP schemas). Keep framework glue thin and generated from the shared registry to remove manual per-tool wrappers.

review::.::holistic::abstraction_fitness.summary: A2A gateway duplicates peer-identity resolution logic across server and adapter layers.

evidence: `A2AGatewayAdapter._resolve_peer` and `GatewayServer._resolve_peer` both implement slug-first/UUID-fallback lookup over parallel peer maps.
Both classes carry `peers` + `peers_by_uuid` state and repeat fallback behavior, increasing maintenance overhead when identifier semantics change.
Integration tests exercise adapter internals (`_get_or_create_room`, peer map mutation) rather than a single boundary object, indicating abstractions are split and leaking.
detail.suggestion: Extract peer identity resolution into one shared component (e.g., `PeerDirectory`) used by both `GatewayServer` and `A2AGatewayAdapter`; pass resolved `PeerRef` objects through callback boundaries instead of repeating lookup rules in each layer.

review::.::holistic::abstraction_fitness.summary: Contact hub orchestration relies on loosely coupled callbacks instead of an explicit integration interface.

evidence: `ContactEventHandler` takes three optional callbacks (`on_broadcast`, `on_hub_event`, `on_hub_init`) and infers behavior from which are set.
`PlatformRuntime._setup_contact_handling` wires those callbacks and coordinates extra state (`_pending_hub_room_id`, queued broadcasts), forming an implicit cross-class protocol.
Tests validate internal sequencing/state transitions (hub readiness, pending broadcasts), which indicates behavior depends on fragile handshake details instead of a single explicit abstraction.
detail.suggestion: Replace callback triad with a typed `ContactEventSink` interface (broadcast, hub-init, hub-inject semantics in one contract) and move readiness/pending-state ownership into one coordinator object to reduce implicit coupling.

review::.::holistic::abstraction_fitness.summary: Example runner path uses layered pass-through wrappers with minimal policy, increasing indirection cost.

evidence: `examples/run_agent.py` defines many `_invoke_*` functions that mostly forward argument bags to `run_*` functions.
`example_runners.py` then defines multiple `run_*_agent` functions that mostly instantiate adapter, call `Agent.create(...)`, and `await agent.run()` with small variations.
The two-layer dispatch adds many function surfaces with little abstraction gain, making behavior changes require touching many thin wrappers.
detail.suggestion: Collapse the wrapper chain into a data-driven runner registry: declarative per-framework adapter factory + optional feature hooks (contacts/streaming/thinking), then one generic invoke path that constructs and runs the agent.

review::.::holistic::test_strategy::orch.summary: Canonical orchestrator agent logic is not directly tested, leaving key routing/status branches unverified.

evidence: `agent.py` implements `stream()` and `_aget_agent_response()` with multiple status mappings (`input_required`, `error`, `completed`) and fallback behavior.
Current orchestrator tests focus on executor outcomes and tool context propagation; they do not exercise `_aget_agent_response` branch matrix or `stream` emission sequencing in the canonical class.
A regression in status mapping or fallback content can pass existing tests while breaking API behavior seen by A2A clients.
detail.suggestion: Add direct unit tests for `OrchestratorAgent` with a mocked LangGraph state/stream to cover each status mapping branch, unknown structured response fallback, interim tool-call event emission, and `close()` behavior.

review::.::holistic::test_strategy::cont.summary: Contact service failure-mode branches are untested because tests validate wrapper happy paths only.

evidence: `ContactService.add_contact`, `remove_contact`, and `respond_contact_request` raise `RuntimeError` when `response.data` is missing.
`tests/runtime/test_contact_tools.py` exercises `ContactTools` with mocked successful responses and input validation, but does not assert behavior when downstream API returns empty/partial payloads.
This leaves critical API failure translation and caller-facing error semantics unprotected.
detail.suggestion: Add service-level tests (or wrapper tests with explicit empty `response.data`) that assert raised exceptions and messages for each failure branch, including list metadata fallbacks when metadata is absent.

review::.::holistic::test_strategy::exam.summary: High-reuse example bootstrap and prompt-building APIs have no regression tests despite being shared entry points.

evidence: `bootstrap.py` encapsulates env loading and required URL validation used across many examples, but no tests were found for missing env/default precedence or `Agent.create` argument wiring.
`characters.py` generates long instruction templates consumed by multiple example integrations, but there are no tests guarding required behavioral constraints in generated prompts.
Given broad reuse, minor edits can silently break many examples without detection.
detail.suggestion: Add lightweight tests for bootstrap config resolution (env/default/error branches) and snapshot/contract tests for prompt builders that assert presence of required sections/constraints without over-coupling to full text.

review::.::holistic::test_strategy::runt.summary: Type/API coverage is split between core and runtime message models, leaving runtime-specific behavior under-tested.

evidence: `runtime/types.py` defines `PlatformMessage.format_for_llm`, `normalize_handle`, and contact synthetic constants used by runtime flows.

`tests/core/test_composition_types.py` validates `core.types.PlatformMessage` behavior (including fallback to `Unknown`), not runtime `PlatformMessage`, which has different implementation assumptions.
This dual-model setup increases risk that tests pass while runtime formatting/normalization behavior diverges from expected API semantics.
detail.suggestion: Add dedicated tests for `thenvoi.runtime.types` (including `normalize_handle` edge cases and runtime `PlatformMessage.format_for_llm`) and document/verify intended parity or intentional divergence from `core.types`.

review::.::holistic::package_organizatio.summary: Contact runtime functionality is split between canonical package and root-level compatibility shims.

evidence: `src/thenvoi/runtime/contact_handler.py` and `src/thenvoi/runtime/contact_service.py` contain only re-exports to `thenvoi.runtime.contacts.*` but still provide first-class import paths.
Tests continue importing the shim path (`from thenvoi.runtime.contact_handler import ContactEventHandler`) instead of canonical `thenvoi.runtime.contacts.contact_handler`.
This creates import-affinity drift inside one domain: both legacy and canonical paths are active, obscuring ownership boundaries.
detail.suggestion: Stage reorg in three steps: (1) create `src/thenvoi/runtime/compat/` and move shim modules there (`contact_handler.py`, `contact_service.py`) to make compatibility explicit; (2) update internal/test imports to canonical paths first (`thenvoi.runtime.contacts.contact_handler` / `.service`) using `rg -n "thenvoi\.runtime\.contact_(handler|service)" src tests`; (3) keep temporary forwarding imports only in `runtime/compat` and remove root-level shims after one release. Validate with `uv run pytest tests/runtime/test_broadcast_changes.py tests/runtime/test_contact_config.py -q` and re-check imports with `rg -n "thenvoi\.runtime\.contact_(handler|service)" src tests`.

review::.::holistic::package_organizatio.summary: Contact-domain runtime tests are stored in a flat runtime test folder instead of a contact-focused subtree.

evidence: Both assigned runtime tests target contact-event behavior/config but live directly under `tests/runtime/`.
Holistic structure reports `tests/runtime/` has 16 files, exceeding the flat-directory overload threshold and mixing multiple runtime concerns.
Because contact code is now explicitly nested under `src/thenvoi/runtime/contacts/`, flat test placement weakens navigability and owner mapping.
detail.suggestion: Stage reorg: (1) create `tests/runtime/contacts/` with `__init__.py`; (2) move `test_broadcast_changes.py` then `test_contact_config.py` into that folder; (3) normalize imports in moved files to canonical contact paths and keep only test-layer fixtures in parent runtime folder. Run `uv run pytest tests/runtime/contacts -q` and `uv run pytest tests/runtime -q` to validate no discovery regressions.

review::.::holistic::package_organizatio.summary: Large `runtime/__init__.py` barrel blends multiple runtime concerns and weakens package boundary discoverability.

evidence: `src/thenvoi/runtime/__init__.py` re-exports many symbols spanning execution, tools, formatting, prompts, trackers, and shutdown.
At the same time, contact functionality is split into dedicated subpackage plus root shim files, so the top-level package surface no longer maps cleanly to directory ownership.
New contributors can discover multiple valid entry points (`runtime.*`, `runtime.contacts.*`, shim modules), increasing boundary ambiguity.
detail.suggestion: Use a staged package-surface reorg: (1) restrict `src/thenvoi/runtime/__init__.py` to stable runtime-core exports only; (2) create explicit subpackage facades (`src/thenvoi/runtime/contacts/__init__.py`) for contact-domain APIs; (3) route legacy imports through `runtime/compat` and document sunset. Validate by running `rg -n "from thenvoi\.runtime import" src tests` to update callers, then `uv run pytest tests/runtime/test_broadcast_changes.py tests/runtime/test_contact_config.py tests/integration/test_hub_room_strategy.py -q`.

review::.::holistic::cross_module_archit.summary: README optional-extra name drifts from pyproject dependency contract.

evidence: README.md instructs `uv add "git+https://github.com/thenvoi/thenvoi-sdk-python.git[pydantic_ai]"`.
pyproject.toml declares `[project.optional-dependencies]` key `pydantic-ai`, not `pydantic_ai`.
detail.suggestion: Align the public install contract in one place: either rename the pyproject extra key to `pydantic_ai` for consistency with examples, or update README commands to `pydantic-ai`; then add a lightweight CI/docs check that validates README extras against `[project.optional-dependencies]` keys.

review::.::holistic::test_strategy::unte.summary: High-fan-in example bootstrap contract is untested despite broad reuse.

evidence: holistic_context marks examples/common/bootstrap.py with fan-in 11 and imports from most example directories.
Search across tests finds no direct coverage for `bootstrap_agent`, `load_runtime_config`, `load_platform_urls`, or `create_agent_from_runtime`.
README positions examples as primary usage paths, making bootstrap behavior part of user-facing contract.
detail.suggestion: Add focused unit tests in `tests/` for bootstrap behavior: env resolution precedence, missing endpoint errors, credential passthrough to `Agent.create`, and dotenv toggle behavior; mock `load_agent_config` and `Agent.create` to keep tests deterministic.

review::.::holistic::test_strategy::inte.summary: Integration fixture contracts rely on manual invocation instead of enforced default gates.

evidence: tests/support/integration/config.py documents real-server dependency and custom cleanup semantics (`--no-clean`, `.env.test`, real credentials).
pyproject.toml pytest config uses `testpaths = ["tests"]` and `addopts = "-v"` without default integration exclusion/marker gate.
Gating exists in helper decorators (`tests/integration/markers.py`), but enforcement depends on individual test usage rather than central policy.
detail.suggestion: Stage a quality-gate hardening plan: (1) add pytest markers registration (`integration`, `requires_api`, `requires_multi_agent`, `requires_user_api`) and set default `-m "not integration"` in pyproject, (2) explicitly mark integration suites and keep env-based skip decorators as secondary checks, (3) add CI commands `uv run pytest tests/ -m "not integration" -v` and `uv run pytest tests/integration/ -m integration -v` with documented env requirements.

review::.::holistic::package_organizatio.summary: Shared integration markers live in scenario test package instead of support boundary.

evidence: holistic_context lists `tests/integration/markers.py` as a shared utility with 12 importers.
`tests/support/integration/config.py` already hosts reusable integration infra and is imported by integration tests, indicating an existing support boundary.
Keeping shared marker contracts in `tests/integration/` mixes reusable infra with scenario tests and obscures where test governance primitives live.
detail.suggestion: Execute a staged reorg: (1) create `tests/support/integration/markers.py` and move marker definitions there, (2) update imports in integration tests to new path, (3) keep a temporary shim `tests/integration/markers.py` re-exporting from support with deprecation note, (4) remove shim after callers migrate. Validate with `uv run ruff check .`, `uv run pyrefly check`, and `uv run pytest tests/ --ignore=tests/integration/ -v`.

review::.::holistic::design_coherence::e.summary: Example agent startup flow is duplicated across frameworks instead of using one composition path.

evidence: Each file repeats the same structural sequence: `load_dotenv()` -> read `THENVOI_WS_URL`/`THENVOI_REST_URL` -> `load_agent_config(...)` -> instantiate framework adapter -> `Agent.create(...)` -> `await agent.run()`.
Differences are mostly adapter class and prompt function, while control flow and error handling are copy-pasted.
The duplication already caused per-file drift (some include KeyboardInterrupt handling and extra logging, others do not).
detail.suggestion: Introduce a shared example runner helper that accepts adapter factory + agent key + optional prompt supplier, and migrate these scripts to thin wrappers. Keep framework-specific behavior in small adapter builder functions.

review::.::holistic::design_coherence::s.summary: Multiple examples rely on `sys.path` mutation, indicating packaging/layout and execution-mode coupling.

evidence: These files call `sys.path.insert(...)` at import time before loading shared prompt/setup modules.
Sibling scripts in the same example families use package-style imports instead, creating two different execution conventions.
Path mutation pushes environment/bootstrap concerns into every script instead of a coherent package/module entrypoint.
detail.suggestion: Make `examples` consistently importable (package execution via `python -m`/`uv run -m`) and remove `sys.path` mutations. Add a single shared import strategy for prompt/setup modules.

review::.::holistic::design_coherence::b.summary: A2A bridge orchestration mixes too many responsibilities in single modules/classes.

evidence: `ThenvoiBridge` in `bridge.py` combines env/config parsing, reconnect policy, signal/shutdown lifecycle, event loop consumption, participant cache management, message deduplication, and dispatch setup.
`MentionRouter.route()` handles filtering, dedup of mentions, lifecycle marking, platform-message normalization, session tracking, fan-out dispatch, and failure collation in one long method.
`ContactEventHandler` similarly combines strategy routing, callback/hub delivery, enrichment, dedup cache, persistence, and broadcast concerns.
detail.suggestion: Split orchestration into focused collaborators: configuration loader, connection supervisor, room/participant cache service, and message dispatch pipeline. Keep top-level classes as coordinators that compose these parts.

review::.::holistic::design_coherence::r.summary: Runner entrypoints duplicate setup pipelines, creating maintainability drift across environments.

evidence: Both Claude runners implement near-identical sequences: load config, resolve role prompt files, combine prompt sections, load custom tools, construct adapter, create agent, run with retry, and cleanup.
Codex runner mirrors the same orchestration structure with separate helper parsing, producing parallel but divergent setup code paths.
Shared runner helpers exist (`runner_core.py`) but core composition logic is still repeated in entrypoint modules.
detail.suggestion: Extract a reusable runner composition layer (config normalization + prompt assembly + tool loading + lifecycle wiring) and keep each runner file focused on adapter-specific parameter mapping only.

review::.::holistic::design_coherence::c.summary: Compatibility shims use inconsistent migration semantics, obscuring deprecation expectations.

evidence: Adapter shims implement lazy `__getattr__` plus deprecation warnings and explicit removal version.
Bridge/runtime shims are silent direct re-exports with no warning/removal metadata.
Consumers of similarly named legacy modules get different migration behavior depending on subsystem.
detail.suggestion: Standardize shim policy through one shared deprecation shim utility (warning behavior, removal metadata, import forwarding) and apply it uniformly across adapters, bridge_core, and runtime shims.

review::.::holistic::ai_generated_debt::.summary: Excessive tutorial-style docstrings/comments drown signal in core runtime and integration files.

evidence: `graph_as_tool` has a very long didactic docstring and repeated explanatory comments (lines 23-122, 132-192) that mostly narrate straightforward operations already visible in code.
`default_messages_state_formatter` uses an extended example-heavy docstring for a small formatter function (`message_formatters.py` lines 37-65).
`Execution` protocol and module header include migration/tutorial narrative mixed into implementation file (`execution.py` lines 1-13, 61-86), creating high reading overhead before behavior.
Top-level package init docstring (`src/thenvoi/__init__.py` lines 1-41) embeds long examples and architecture narration in import-surface code.
detail.suggestion: Trim these to concise contract-focused docs: keep 1-3 sentence module intent, move tutorials/migration guides to dedicated docs (e.g., `docs/`), and remove comments that only restate code steps.

review::.::holistic::ai_generated_debt::.summary: Demo orchestrator compatibility files are no-op re-export wrappers that add maintenance noise.

evidence: Each shim file imports symbols from canonical module and re-exports via `__all__` without added behavior or boundary policy.
`agent.py` and `agent_executor.py` are effectively import aliases only.
`remote_agent.py` repeats a broad symbol list solely to forward names.
detail.suggestion: Collapse these into a single compatibility entrypoint or remove shims and update callers to canonical imports; if compatibility is required, centralize aliasing in one module with deprecation warnings and sunset timeline.

review::.::holistic::ai_generated_debt::.summary: Example logging setup modules are duplicated boilerplate differing only by namespace string.

evidence: All files contain the same structure: import `build_logging_setup` then assign `setup_logging = build_logging_setup("<name>")`.
Most docstrings are near-identical ('Shared logging configuration for examples' variants), reinforcing copy-template churn.
detail.suggestion: Replace per-framework files with one shared helper that accepts framework id at call site, or generate these aliases from a small registry to avoid repeated handwritten stubs.

subjective_review::.::holistic_stale.summary: Codebase changed significantly since holistic review (424→520 files) — re-review recommended

resolution_attestation.text: Holistic review refreshed; coverage marker superseded

review::.::holistic::abstraction_fitness.summary: A single kitchen-sink tools protocol forces unrelated capabilities into one dependency surface.

evidence: AgentToolsProtocol includes message, participant, contacts, memory, schema, and dispatch methods in one Protocol instead of bounded capability contracts.
FakeAgentTools must implement the same broad surface, including memory and contacts methods, to satisfy adapter tests.
Adapter tests build large mocks with many method stubs even when individual scenarios only need a few methods.
detail.suggestion: Split AgentToolsProtocol into smaller protocols (for example MessagingTools, ParticipantTools, ContactTools, MemoryTools, ToolSchemaProvider), then type each adapter and runtime path to the minimal protocol it actually needs; keep AgentTools as a concrete aggregate implementation.

review::.::holistic::abstraction_fitness.summary: Tool invocation logic is duplicated across incompatible dispatch abstractions.

evidence: AgentTools.execute_tool_call maintains a hand-written dispatch dictionary mapping tool names to lambdas.
PydanticAIAdapter generates tool functions from TOOL_BINDINGS and routes execution through invoke_platform_tool instead of execute_tool_call.
AnthropicAdapter and CodexAdapter call tools.execute_tool_call from their tool loops, so behavior depends on adapter path.
detail.suggestion: Consolidate all tool execution through one canonical dispatcher (preferably bindings-driven), and make adapter integrations call that shared path so adding/changing tools is single-source and behaviorally consistent.

review::.::holistic::abstraction_fitness.summary: Multiple adapters reimplement the same bootstrap/history/context choreography instead of sharing a reusable abstraction.

evidence: Adapters each implement near-identical per-room history initialization for is_session_bootstrap and safety fallback when room state is missing.
Adapters repeat participants/contacts message injection patterns with provider-specific formatting differences, creating parallel logic branches.
Adapter test suites repeatedly validate the same lifecycle behavior (history bootstrap, metadata injection), reflecting duplicated contract logic in each implementation.
detail.suggestion: Move shared session lifecycle behavior into a reusable base/mixin (room state lifecycle + metadata injection policy hooks), leaving only provider-specific serialization and API calls in individual adapters.

review::.::holistic::abstraction_fitness.summary: A2A GatewayAdapter inherits the generic adapter contract but operates through a separate REST-first control plane.

evidence: Gateway adapter docstring explicitly states AgentToolsProtocol is not used for core operations; it instantiates and owns AsyncRestClient directly.
on_message includes a tools parameter but comments note it is intentionally unused because room operations are done via self._rest.
Tests pass FakeAgentTools to on_message but assertions focus on adapter-owned REST and pending-task state, showing the inherited tool contract is largely ceremonial.
detail.suggestion: Introduce a dedicated gateway/bridge adapter interface (or separate runtime path) that models HTTP-request-driven, REST-bound orchestration directly, instead of forcing it through SimpleAdapter’s room-tool contract.

review::.::holistic::abstraction_fitness.summary: Contact handler preserves compatibility wrappers and private fields primarily for tests, leaking internals into the effective API surface.

evidence: contact_handler exposes compatibility aliases/wrappers such as _hub_room_id, _cache_request_info, _get_cached_request_info, and _fetch_request_details with comments indicating test/introspection compatibility.
Tests assert private internals (e.g., handler._processed_events size, handler._get_dedup_key output), coupling test stability to implementation details.
This creates pressure to retain legacy private structure even as collaborators (dedup cache/request info store) are extracted.
detail.suggestion: Replace private compatibility hooks with an explicit narrow diagnostics interface (or public stats DTO), migrate tests to public behavior assertions, and remove private wrapper methods once callers are updated.

review::.::holistic::test_strategy::sess.summary: Session-store observability and threshold-reset logic is untested, leaving a high-cardinality operational path unvalidated.

evidence: InMemorySessionStore._evict_expired() mutates _high_session_warned and conditionally emits logger.warning when count >= 10_000, with a separate reset branch when count falls below threshold.
This branch is operationally significant (log-noise control and alert behavior) but current coverage focuses on CRUD/TTL outcomes and does not assert warning-once / reset-on-recovery behavior.
detail.suggestion: Add focused unit tests that populate the store across the threshold and back below it, asserting warning emission count and reset semantics; isolate logger with caplog or a patched logger to verify behavior deterministically.

review::.::holistic::test_strategy::hand.summary: The BaseHandler API contract lacks contract-level tests with concrete implementations, so signature drift can pass tests and fail at runtime.

evidence: BaseHandler.handle() requires (message, mentioned_agent, tools) and is the core router integration contract.
Routing tests commonly use AsyncMock handlers, which accept arbitrary kwargs and therefore do not fail when concrete handler signatures diverge from the protocol contract.
detail.suggestion: Introduce contract tests using minimal concrete handler classes (not generic AsyncMock) and run them through routing dispatch, including at least one intentionally malformed handler to verify failures are surfaced predictably.

review::.::holistic::test_strategy::defa.summary: Default test gate excludes integration coverage for production-ready adapter contracts.

evidence: pyproject.toml default addopts uses "-m 'not integration'", so integration checks are not part of default test execution.
README.md labels many frameworks/adapters as production ready, but integration execution is documented as a separate, optional step.

examples/common/bootstrap.py and examples/prompts/characters.py are high-importer example surfaces used as cross-module entry points, yet they are outside pytest testpaths and not part of a documented required contract suite.
detail.suggestion: Define and enforce a mandatory contract test matrix per supported adapter in the default CI gate (or a required secondary gate), and document it in README alongside the support matrix so production-ready claims are backed by required cross-module verification.

review::.::holistic::test_strategy::inte.summary: Integration fixtures encode persistent state and timing assumptions that reduce repeatability.

evidence: tests/support/integration/config.py test_chat fixture explicitly does not clean up chats after yielding, causing resource accumulation across runs.
cleanup_contact_state in tests/support/integration/config.py relies on a fixed sleep (settle_delay_s=0.3), which is susceptible to environment timing variance.
README.md promotes repeated integration runs and also documents no-clean behavior, but there is no corresponding deterministic isolation strategy described for accumulated server-side state.
detail.suggestion: Introduce deterministic test isolation primitives (unique resource namespaces per test run, robust polling/assertion for eventual consistency instead of fixed sleep, and periodic cleanup tooling for undeletable resources) and codify this policy in integration test docs.

review::.::holistic::test_strategy::manu.summary: Live API fixture usage depends on per-test discipline instead of centralized enforcement.

evidence: tests/support/integration/markers.py provides reusable decorators, but adoption is voluntary at call sites.
tests/support/integration/config.py fixtures create real AsyncRestClient instances and perform network operations without asserting integration marking context.
With pyproject.toml default marker filtering, a test that forgets integration marking can still run in default suites if credentials are present, creating accidental external coupling.
detail.suggestion: Add a central pytest guard (e.g., in plugin/hooks) that fails tests using integration fixtures unless explicitly marked integration/requires_* and run under integration mode, so policy is enforced mechanically rather than by convention.

review::.::holistic::design_coherence::a.summary: Framework adapters reimplement the same session-bootstrap/history pipeline with divergent control flow, creating behavior drift and hard-to-apply cross-adapter fixes.

evidence: Each adapter manually performs `is_session_bootstrap` handling and room-scoped history initialization (`_message_history`/similar) instead of delegating to a shared lifecycle helper.
Each adapter has separate fallback behavior when room state is missing on non-bootstrap paths (e.g., initialize empty history), so consistency depends on many independent implementations.
Cross-cutting concerns (history injection, participants/contacts handling, cleanup expectations) are duplicated in adapter-local logic rather than centralized policy.
detail.suggestion: Extract a shared adapter session pipeline (e.g., in `SimpleAdapter`) that owns room-state initialization, bootstrap hydration policy, and system-message staging; keep framework adapters focused on provider-specific request/response translation only.

review::.::holistic::design_coherence::s.summary: System metadata updates are encoded differently across adapters, producing inconsistent conversational semantics across frameworks.

evidence: `ParlantAdapter` rewrites the main user message by prepending `[System Update]: ...` blocks.
`LangGraphAdapter`, `AnthropicAdapter`, `PydanticAIAdapter`, and `ClaudeSDKAdapter` append separate `[System]: ...` messages/events instead.
The same platform signal (`participants_msg`/`contacts_msg`) therefore changes ordering and role semantics depending on adapter choice.
detail.suggestion: Define one canonical system-update contract (ordering, role, and prefix format) in shared integration utilities and reuse it in every adapter via a single helper.

review::.::holistic::design_coherence::c.summary: Deprecation compatibility shims are manually duplicated across many modules, creating a brittle migration surface with high drift risk.

evidence: Multiple shim modules repeat the same pattern (`REMOVAL_VERSION`, `_FORWARD_MAP`, `make_deprecated_getattr`) with package-specific manual string maps.
Canonical-path mapping knowledge is distributed across many files instead of a single migration manifest.
Removal-version updates and symbol moves require synchronized edits in many shim files, increasing chance of stale forwards.
detail.suggestion: Consolidate shims behind a central deprecation registry (or generated shim module) so forward maps and removal policy are defined once and consumed by package-level facades.

review::.::holistic::design_coherence::a.summary: A2A gateway orchestration mixes transport, routing, context persistence, and task correlation responsibilities in one adapter boundary.

evidence: `A2AGatewayAdapter` owns peer discovery, room/context mapping, pending-task state, REST messaging, and HTTP server startup/shutdown.
`GatewayServer` also carries protocol branching and serialization concerns for both legacy SSE and JSON-RPC endpoints, tightly coupled to adapter callback behavior.
Examples instantiate the adapter as the integration entrypoint, so this broad surface becomes the default extension point and raises modification risk for unrelated changes.
detail.suggestion: Split gateway responsibilities into explicit components (peer discovery, room-context/session manager, task correlator, transport endpoint layer) and keep `A2AGatewayAdapter` as a thin composition root.

review::.::holistic::abstraction_fitness.summary: Gateway adapter exposes test-anchored compatibility wrappers/aliases that duplicate collaborator APIs instead of maintaining one stable abstraction boundary.

evidence: `A2AGatewayAdapter.__init__` publishes internal alias fields (`_context_to_room`, `_room_participants`, `_pending_tasks`) from collaborator objects.

Adapter defines wrapper methods (`_get_or_create_room`, `_rehydrate`, `_create_task`, `_translate_to_a2a`, `_emit_context_event`) that only delegate to `GatewaySessionManager`/`GatewayTaskCorrelator`.

Tests call wrapper methods and assert alias internals directly (e.g., `test_adapter.py` checks `_context_to_room`/`_room_participants` and calls `_get_or_create_room`, `_rehydrate`, `_translate_to_a2a`).
detail.suggestion: Introduce a single explicit facade contract for gateway state/operations (or test collaborators directly), then deprecate and remove private compatibility aliases/wrappers in one migration to eliminate duplicate API surfaces.

review::.::holistic::abstraction_fitness.summary: The adapter tool interface is over-broad, forcing consumers and test doubles to implement capabilities they do not use.

evidence: `AgentToolsProtocol` composes messaging, participant, contact, memory, schema-provider, and dispatch contracts into one required type.
`SimpleAdapter.on_message` requires `AgentToolsProtocol`, propagating the full surface to every adapter subclass regardless of actual needs.

`FakeAgentTools` implements the whole surface (including memory/contact/schema methods) to satisfy typing/test use, while `PydanticAIAdapter` separately defines a narrower `PydanticAIToolsProtocol` indicating real usage is smaller.
detail.suggestion: Make `SimpleAdapter` generic over a tool-capabilities protocol (or split `on_message` contracts per adapter family) so each adapter and test fake binds only to required capabilities.

review::.::holistic::abstraction_fitness.summary: Bridge injects hidden cross-object mutation through `__setattr__`, creating implicit coupling between orchestrator and collaborators.

evidence: `ThenvoiBridge.__setattr__` intercepts `_link` assignment and mutates collaborator state (`router._link`, `participant_directory.set_link`, `event_dispatcher.set_link`).
Tests repeatedly replace `bridge._link` directly with mocks, relying on this non-obvious side effect path instead of explicit dependency APIs.
detail.suggestion: Replace `__setattr__` hook with an explicit `set_link()`/rebind method on the bridge and collaborator interfaces, and update tests to use the explicit API to remove hidden mutation paths.

review::.::holistic::abstraction_fitness.summary: Execution is declared as a protocol but key runtime extension points still depend on concrete `ExecutionContext`, reducing abstraction value.

evidence: `execution.py` defines `Execution` protocol, but `ExecutionHandler` is typed as `Callable[[ExecutionContext, PlatformEvent], ...]` rather than `Execution`.
`PlatformRuntime.start` requires `on_execute: Callable[[ExecutionContext, PlatformEvent], Awaitable[None]]`, so callers are coupled to concrete context type.
Runtime tests validate callback plumbing around this concrete shape rather than a protocol contract.
detail.suggestion: Retype callback boundaries to the `Execution` protocol (or a smaller execution-callback protocol) and expose only required context methods via that interface, keeping `ExecutionContext` as an implementation detail.

review::.::holistic::test_strategy::over.summary: Bootstrap tests are over-mocked and provide weak confidence for a high-fan-in API path.

evidence: `bootstrap_agent` behavior is asserted by patching both internal steps (`load_runtime_config` and `create_agent_from_runtime`) and checking call wiring, not real config-resolution behavior.

`load_runtime_config` and `create_agent_from_runtime` tests each replace core collaborators, so regressions in integration between URL resolution, credential resolution, and agent creation can pass unit tests.
Packet context flags this file as over-mocked (`15 mocks vs 13 assertions`) while the module is widely reused.
detail.suggestion: Add at least one integration-style contract test for `bootstrap_agent` that uses real `load_runtime_config` logic and controlled environment variables, mocking only external boundary effects (for example `Agent.create`) to assert end-to-end resolved runtime values passed into agent creation.

review::.::holistic::test_strategy::miss.summary: No integration test verifies router dispatch behavior against session TTL/activity semantics.

evidence: `session.py` defines activity-sensitive eviction (`_evict_expired`) and refresh-on-access behavior in `get_or_create`.
Router flow in `src/thenvoi/integrations/a2a_bridge/router.py` depends on `await self._session_store.get_or_create(room_id)` during each routed message.

Existing tests split concerns (`tests/bridge/test_session.py` for store internals, `tests/bridge/test_router.py` for routing), but there is no test combining a TTL-configured store with router message flow to verify session survival/replacement behavior at module boundaries.
detail.suggestion: Add a cross-module async test that wires `MentionRouter` with `InMemorySessionStore(session_ttl=...)`, simulates activity/expiry transitions, and asserts routing updates session state as expected across consecutive messages.

review::.::holistic::test_strategy::cano.summary: Tests target deprecated `bridge_core` compatibility imports instead of canonical `a2a_bridge` imports.

evidence: Bridge tests import `InMemorySessionStore`/`SessionData` from `bridge_core.session`, not from `thenvoi.integrations.a2a_bridge.session`.
`thenvoi-bridge/bridge_core/session.py` and `handler.py` are compatibility shims using `make_registered_module_shim`.
Coverage through deprecated aliases can miss canonical import-surface regressions (package export/wiring errors) until runtime migration paths change.
detail.suggestion: Add canonical-path import tests (or migrate existing bridge tests) to assert `thenvoi.integrations.a2a_bridge.session` and `.handler` imports and symbol contracts directly, while keeping a minimal separate test for compatibility shims.

review::.::holistic::test_strategy::mark.summary: Integration marker policy is duplicated across config and marker helpers, creating governance drift risk for live-fixture gating.

evidence: `pyproject.toml` declares marker names (`requires_api`, `requires_multi_agent`, `requires_user_api`).
`tests/support/integration/markers.py` defines decorators for the same marker set.
`tests/support/integration/config.py` hardcodes required marker names again in `_LIVE_INTEGRATION_FIXTURE_MARKERS` as raw strings.
No single source enforces synchronization of declared markers, decorators, and fixture-policy mapping.
detail.suggestion: Create one canonical marker-policy registry (e.g., a typed mapping module) consumed by both marker decorators and live-fixture enforcement, and add a contract test that validates registry entries match `pyproject.toml` marker declarations.

review::.::holistic::test_strategy::clea.summary: Integration cleanup swallows broad exceptions, which can mask API regressions and leave state-dependent flakiness undetected.

evidence: `cleanup_contact_state` in `tests/support/integration/config.py` catches `Exception` repeatedly for contact/remove/cancel/reject flows and only logs debug messages.
Most cleanup operations continue after failures, so tests can proceed in partially dirty state.
Marker enforcement ensures test intent (`integration`/credentials) but does not require cleanup success as a quality gate.
detail.suggestion: Narrow exception handling to known transient API errors, aggregate cleanup failures, and fail or xfail when settlement cannot be achieved under strict mode; expose a fixture option to allow best-effort cleanup only when explicitly requested.

review::.::holistic::test_strategy::even.summary: Shared event factories favor permissive defaults over explicit contract checks, reducing sensitivity to runtime event-schema drift.

evidence: `tests/support/events.py` builders accept `**kwargs` and populate many defaults (including fixed timestamps), which encourages tests to validate happy-path shapes only.
`pyproject.toml` excludes `tests/**` from pyrefly checks, so type-level drift inside test helpers is not statically gated.
`README.md` advertises multiple production-ready integrations, increasing the need for strict cross-event contract coverage rather than permissive helper defaults.
detail.suggestion: Add explicit schema-contract tests for each event helper (required fields, invalid/unknown fields, versioned payload compatibility) and consider enabling targeted static checks for `tests/support/events.py` to catch helper drift early.

review::.::holistic::design_coherence::t.summary: Platform tool contracts are duplicated across runtime and adapter layers, creating drift-prone behavior and inconsistent error semantics.

evidence: `execute_tool_call` in `runtime/tools.py` defines its own name→call mapping and error strings.
`runtime/tool_bridge.py` redefines tool-name normalization/error formatting and also includes a legacy fallback dispatch chain.
Framework integrations still maintain per-framework wrappers around the same tool set (`claude_sdk/tools.py` and `parlant/tools.py`), including additional local parsing/formatting logic.
Tool ordering/constants are repeated (`PLATFORM_*_TOOL_ORDER` and `_LANGGRAPH_*_TOOL_ORDER`) instead of derived from a single registry.
detail.suggestion: Introduce one canonical tool registry object (schema, normalization, dispatch, error mapping, ordering) in runtime; make framework integrations consume that registry through a thin adapter interface and remove per-framework duplicated tool wiring.

review::.::holistic::design_coherence::a.summary: Simple and control-plane adapter bases duplicate lifecycle/conversion flow, increasing maintenance divergence risk.

evidence: Both base classes store `history_converter`, `agent_name`, `agent_description` and implement nearly identical `on_started` behavior with converter propagation.
Both implement `on_event` with the same conversion pattern (`inp.history.convert(...)` fallback to raw) then call `on_message`.
Both expose identical no-op `on_cleanup` hook with separate implementations.
detail.suggestion: Extract shared adapter lifecycle into one reusable mixin/base (or unify base classes with a typed tool-capability mode flag) so conversion/startup/cleanup semantics are implemented once and specialized only where behavior truly differs.

review::.::holistic::design_coherence::l.summary: Preprocessing depends on an integrations utility for core runtime participant formatting, weakening architectural boundaries.

evidence: `DefaultPreprocessor` imports `check_and_format_participants` from `integrations/base.py` even though preprocessing is part of runtime message pipeline.
`integrations/base.py` utility requires `ExecutionContext` semantics and runtime formatter behavior, so integration layer acts as a generic-runtime utility bucket.
This makes dependency direction ambiguous: runtime/preprocessing relies on integration package for behavior that is not integration-specific.
detail.suggestion: Move participant change formatting helper into a runtime/preprocessing module (or dedicated runtime utilities package) and keep `integrations/base.py` scoped to true integration translation concerns.

review::.::holistic::design_coherence::r.summary: Docker runner bootstrap policy is split across runner scripts, causing repeated orchestration and inconsistent configuration conventions.

evidence: Both runner scripts repeat startup orchestration steps (read env/config, initialize repo, build prompt, create adapter, create agent, run lifecycle) with runner-specific parsing in file-local helpers.
`runner_core.py` already centralizes large parts of this lifecycle, but policy details (env key precedence, adapter config parsing) remain spread across runner files.

Codex runner introduces several parser helpers (`_parse_transport`, `_parse_approval_mode`, `_parse_reasoning_effort`) while Claude runner uses direct env reads, leading to non-uniform validation patterns.
detail.suggestion: Create a shared typed runner-config composition layer (per-adapter schema + common bootstrap template) so each runner only declares adapter-specific fields while startup flow and validation conventions stay centralized.

review::.::holistic::design_coherence::t.summary: Adapter tests reimplement tool dispatch logic inline, coupling tests to duplicated behavior instead of one shared contract helper.

evidence: `tests/adapters/test_crewai_adapter.py` defines `_execute_tool_call` with manual tool-name branching mirroring production dispatch behavior.
`tests/adapters/test_codex_adapter.py` defines custom fake tool schemas and behavior-specific fakes, bypassing shared runtime tool bridge contracts.
When production tool mappings/error contracts evolve, duplicated test-side dispatch can silently diverge from runtime semantics.
detail.suggestion: Provide a shared testing utility that uses the same canonical tool registry/dispatch path as production, and update adapter tests to use it instead of local if/elif dispatch replicas.

review::.::holistic::abstraction_fitness.summary: Example runner abstraction is incompatible with ParlantAdapter’s actual constructor contract.

evidence: `run_parlant_agent` constructs `ParlantAdapter(model=..., guidelines=..., enable_execution_reporting=...)` in `src/thenvoi/testing/example_runners.py`.
`ParlantAdapter.__init__` in `src/thenvoi/adapters/parlant.py` requires `server` and `parlant_agent` and has no `model`, `guidelines`, or `enable_execution_reporting` parameters.
detail.suggestion: Unify the public adapter contract: either (a) update `run_parlant_agent` to instantiate a real Parlant server/agent and pass `server`/`parlant_agent`, or (b) introduce a supported factory/wrapper in the adapter layer that accepts model-level config and internally provisions Parlant objects. Keep one canonical initialization path and remove stale kwargs.

review::.::holistic::abstraction_fitness.summary: Full `AgentToolsProtocol` is used where narrower tool contracts would reduce coupling.

evidence: `AgentToolsProtocol` composes six independent capabilities (messaging, participants, contacts, memory, schema export, dispatch) in `src/thenvoi/core/protocols.py`.
Adapters like Anthropic/LangGraph/Parlant type `tools` as full `AgentToolsProtocol` in `on_message`, forcing dependency on unrelated capabilities.
`PydanticAIAdapter` already demonstrates a narrower contract via `PydanticAIToolsProtocol` (messaging + dispatch), showing this decoupling is viable but inconsistently applied.
detail.suggestion: Define and adopt minimal per-adapter tool protocols (e.g., Messaging+Dispatch, Messaging+Schema, etc.) and update adapter signatures/tests/fakes to those slices. Keep `AgentToolsProtocol` only as a maximal implementation contract, not the default dependency type.

review::.::holistic::abstraction_fitness.summary: Codex test doubles violate the tool-schema protocol signature, weakening interface guarantees.

evidence: `ToolSchemaProviderProtocol.get_openai_tool_schemas` is declared as keyword-only `include_memory` in `src/thenvoi/core/protocols.py`.

`ToolSchemaFakeTools.get_openai_tool_schemas(self)` in `tests/adapters/test_codex_adapter.py` and `_ToolSchemaFakeTools.get_openai_tool_schemas(self)` in `tests/integrations/codex/test_adapter_e2e.py` omit that parameter.
`FakeAgentTools` in `src/thenvoi/testing/fake_tools.py` follows the full signature, so codex-specific fakes are inconsistent with the shared contract.
detail.suggestion: Align all test doubles to the protocol signature (`*, include_memory: bool = False`) and add a small protocol-conformance test for codex fakes so signature drift is caught before behavior tests run.

review::.::holistic::abstraction_fitness.summary: Adapter configuration abstractions are inconsistent, creating high-entropy constructor APIs.

evidence: `CodexAdapter` encapsulates options in `CodexAdapterConfig` (many fields but one bounded object) in `src/thenvoi/adapters/codex.py`.
Anthropic/ClaudeSDK/CrewAI/PydanticAI adapters expose many optional constructor args directly, mixing feature toggles, compatibility knobs, and provider settings in each class.
`CrewAIAdapter` still carries deprecated `system_prompt` compatibility behavior in constructor path, further widening and aging the surface.
detail.suggestion: Standardize on per-adapter typed config objects (Pydantic/dataclass) plus thin constructor overloads for backward compatibility; move deprecated aliases into explicit migration shims. This keeps adapter constructors stable and makes extension pressure explicit.

review::.::holistic::test_strategy::miss.summary: Bootstrap path couples env loading, credential lookup, and agent construction, making critical setup behavior hard to unit test deterministically.

evidence: `load_platform_urls()` conditionally calls `load_dotenv()` directly, tying behavior to process environment state.
`load_runtime_config()` always calls `resolve_agent_credentials(agent_key)` with no injected resolver.
`create_agent_from_runtime()` directly invokes `Agent.create(...)`, requiring heavy patching to test creation failure/success branches.
detail.suggestion: Introduce injectable collaborators (env loader, credential resolver, agent factory) via parameters with defaults; then add focused unit tests for each failure/success branch without monkeypatching global functions.

review::.::holistic::test_strategy::time.summary: Session expiration logic depends on wall-clock reads in method bodies, which encourages brittle timing-dependent tests for TTL behavior.

evidence: `_evict_expired()` and `get_or_create()` both call `datetime.now(timezone.utc)` inline.
Eviction is opportunistic (runs on access methods), so tests must orchestrate call ordering and time progression to assert expiry.
No clock abstraction or overridable time source exists to make expiration scenarios deterministic.
detail.suggestion: Inject a clock callable (defaulting to UTC now) into `InMemorySessionStore` and centralize time acquisition through it; write deterministic tests by supplying a fake clock and explicit timestamps.

review::.::holistic::test_strategy::hand.summary: Handler API exposes only side effects and no outcome contract, increasing coupling and making tests assert implementation details.

evidence: `BaseHandler.handle(...)` returns `None`, so tests cannot assert structured routing/processing results.
The only observable output implied by the protocol is behavior through `AgentTools`, which usually requires mocks/spies and call-order assertions.
No standardized result/error object is defined to validate behavior independently of concrete tool invocation details.
detail.suggestion: Define a small handler result contract (e.g., handled/ignored/error metadata) and update implementations/tests to assert result semantics first, with tool-call assertions only where integration behavior is required.

review::.::holistic::test_strategy::prod.summary: Production-readiness claims are broader than the default quality gate coverage for integration contracts.

evidence: README.md labels many framework adapters as "Production ready".
pyproject.toml sets pytest `addopts = "-v -m 'not integration'"`, which excludes live integration checks by default.
tests/support/integration/config.py contains real API fixtures and policy enforcement intended for integration-mode runs.
detail.suggestion: Introduce a required CI contract matrix that runs integration suites (or targeted smoke contracts) per supported adapter extra, and align README production claims to that matrix (including explicit pass criteria and cadence).

review::.::holistic::test_strategy::inte.summary: Live fixture marker policy is robust but opt-in, leaving frequent test runs blind to integration contract regressions.

evidence: tests/support/integration/config.py enforces marker discipline in `pytest_runtest_setup` for live fixtures.
tests/support/integration/markers.py defines required marker decorators for env-gated integration tests.
pyproject.toml defaults to `not integration`, so these governance checks are bypassed unless explicitly enabled.
detail.suggestion: Add a mandatory lightweight integration contract stage (e.g., nightly/PR label-triggered) and fail builds when integration-marker policy violations exist, instead of relying on local opt-in execution.

review::.::holistic::test_strategy::live.summary: Integration fixtures depend on external ordering/state, increasing flake risk for contract validation.

evidence: In `test_chat`, fixture behavior chooses the first peer from `list_agent_peers()` (`peers_response.data[0]`) when available.
In `test_peer_id`, fixture falls back to first peer if filtering cannot exclude owner.
README.md promotes broad framework usage, but this state-dependent selection weakens repeatable governance checks across environments.
detail.suggestion: Create deterministic fixture setup for integration tests (dedicated seeded peers/agents per run ID, explicit selection predicates, and fail-fast when preconditions are not met) to reduce environmental flakiness.

review::.::holistic::test_strategy::docs.summary: Docs-facing runtime contracts in heavily reused example modules are not paired with explicit contract tests in this governance slice.

evidence: examples/common/bootstrap.py defines shared bootstrap behavior for runtime credentials/endpoints.
examples/prompts/characters.py encodes strict behavioral rules (message limits, mission flow, tool usage constraints).
tests/support/events.py provides event factories that could support behavior-contract verification, but no explicit prompt/bootstrap contract assertions are present in this batch.
detail.suggestion: Add focused contract tests for example bootstrap and prompt invariants (credential resolution, endpoint precedence, required prompt sections/limits) and wire them into the default non-integration suite.

review::.::holistic::design_coherence::c.summary: Codex adapter has become a multi-role orchestrator that mixes unrelated responsibilities and raises change risk.

evidence: One class handles RPC client bootstrapping, thread resume/start, turn loop, server request handling, local slash-commands, manual approval bookkeeping, model fallback, sandbox normalization, and event emission.

The adapter exposes many narrowly scoped private helpers that still depend on shared mutable adapter state (`_room_threads`, `_pending_approvals`, `_selected_model`, `_task_titles_by_id`), indicating high internal coupling instead of modular ownership.
Large behavior-specific test surface in `tests/adapters/test_codex_adapter.py` reflects this concentration by validating many orthogonal concerns through one entrypoint.
detail.suggestion: Split Codex behavior into composable collaborators (session lifecycle, command/approval controller, turn-event reducer, and policy/config normalizers) and keep `CodexAdapter` as orchestration glue with a narrow state model.

review::.::holistic::design_coherence::c.note: not reported in latest holistic re-import

review::.::holistic::design_coherence::a.summary: Framework adapters duplicate the same room bootstrap/system-update/history choreography with per-file drift.

evidence: Anthropic, PydanticAI, and CrewAI adapters each stage per-room history, inject participant/contact system updates, then run framework-specific execution loops with similar error-report pathways.
LangGraph adapter reimplements bootstrap-once logic (`_bootstrapped_rooms`) and system update injection independently, instead of using a shared adapter lifecycle component.
`AdapterLifecycleBase` centralizes only minimal conversion dispatch, leaving repeated runtime session policy spread across adapter files.
detail.suggestion: Introduce a shared room-session orchestration layer (bootstrap policy, update injection, error reporting contract) consumed by framework adapters, with framework-specific code limited to request/response translation.

review::.::holistic::design_coherence::r.summary: Runtime execution context and platform runtime each aggregate too many lifecycle concerns, blurring ownership boundaries.

evidence: `ExecutionContext` handles queue processing, sync reconciliation with `/next`, retry accounting, stale-message recovery, participant cache, context hydration, and system-message injection in one class.
`PlatformRuntime` coordinates link startup/shutdown, metadata retrieval, contact strategy wiring, contact subscription state, broadcast queueing, and hub-room injection flow.
Contact handling integration requires cross-object callbacks (`_queue_broadcast`, `_inject_hub_event`, `_inject_hub_system_prompt`) that couple runtime orchestration to contact strategy internals.
detail.suggestion: Separate runtime into explicit components (message-sync engine, execution loop, participant/context cache, and contact orchestration facade) with clear handoff contracts to reduce cross-component callback coupling.

review::.::holistic::design_coherence::r.note: not reported in latest holistic re-import

review::.::holistic::design_coherence::c.summary: WebSocket channel APIs are mechanically repeated across streaming and link layers, increasing drift potential.

evidence: `WebSocketClient` repeats near-identical `join_*`/`leave_*` methods that differ mostly by topic string and handler map.
`ThenvoiLink` adds another repetitive layer for subscribe/unsubscribe and per-event wrapper methods (`_on_*`) that mostly translate payloads into event objects and enqueue them.
Duplicated choreography requires parallel edits whenever channel/event shapes change.
detail.suggestion: Move to a declarative channel registry (topic template + payload model + event constructor) and generate join/leave + dispatch wiring from one table shared by both layers.

review::.::holistic::design_coherence::r.summary: Runner startup logic is duplicated across Docker/example entrypoints with inconsistent defaults and contracts.

evidence: All three runners perform similar config-path/env resolution, mount validation, prompt composition, adapter creation, and lifecycle startup with hand-maintained variations.
Behavior diverges across files (mount requirements, role/workspace resolution style, inclusion of repo-init context bundle), despite serving the same startup shape.
This creates a maintenance pattern where feature additions/fixes must be manually replicated across entrypoints.
detail.suggestion: Define a single declarative runner spec and shared builder in `runner_core` that each entrypoint configures via small profile objects rather than duplicating startup flow.

review::.::holistic::design_coherence::g.summary: Gateway history participant reconstruction relies on a sender-type convention that drifts from other runtime usage.

evidence: `GatewayHistoryConverter` tracks participants only when `sender_type == "agent"` (lowercase), while other runtime paths compare against `"Agent"` (capitalized) for agent-origin filtering.
No gateway-focused integration assertion in `tests/integration/test_history_converters.py` covers this sender-type normalization boundary, so convention drift can persist silently.
This is a coherence issue at the contract boundary: converter behavior depends on implicit casing assumptions not normalized in one place.
detail.suggestion: Normalize sender type through a shared canonical helper (case-insensitive enum-style normalization) and add converter integration tests that cover mixed-case sender types from platform history.
