review::.::holistic::abstraction_fitness.summary: Per-adapter tool protocol aliases create parallel interfaces with minimal semantic differences, increasing maintenance without adding real extension value.

evidence: `LangGraphToolsProtocol`, `PydanticAIToolsProtocol`, and `ParlantToolsProtocol` each mostly restate `MessagingToolsProtocol + ToolDispatchProtocol` rather than defining materially distinct contracts.
`AnthropicToolsProtocol` adds one schema method, but still follows the same per-adapter pattern, producing N parallel protocol types for one underlying tool surface.
This fragmentation propagates into adapter generics and test doubles instead of a single stable capability model.
detail.suggestion: Replace adapter-local protocol aliases with a small shared set of capability protocols (e.g., `DispatchTools`, `MessagingDispatchTools`, `AnthropicSchemaTools`) in `core/protocols.py`, and have adapters type against those shared contracts directly.

review::.::holistic::abstraction_fitness.summary: Adapter config objects and `from_config` constructors are repeated as mostly mechanical pass-through wrappers, causing change amplification across frameworks.

evidence: Each adapter defines a dataclass config and a `from_config` classmethod that forwards nearly every field one-to-one into `__init__`.
Behavioral policy is rarely added in `from_config`; most code is field mapping boilerplate, so API evolution requires synchronized edits in multiple adapters and tests.
Test coverage includes repeated `from_config` checks per adapter, reflecting structural duplication rather than distinct domain behavior.
detail.suggestion: Introduce a shared config-loading mechanism (e.g., `@classmethod from_mapping` on a common adapter base or a reusable mapper utility) and keep adapter-specific config only for truly unique fields/validation.

review::.::holistic::abstraction_fitness.summary: Contact handling relies on callback indirection between runtime and handler, creating a leaky boundary and wrapper chain with temporal coupling.

evidence: `PlatformRuntime._setup_contact_handling()` injects callbacks (`_queue_broadcast`, `_inject_hub_event`, `_inject_hub_system_prompt`) into `ContactEventHandler`.
`ContactEventHandler` then routes through sink callbacks to call back into runtime-owned execution contexts for hub-room operations, instead of targeting a dedicated runtime-facing interface.
The behavior depends on ordering (`initialize_hub_room`, runtime execution creation, `mark_hub_room_ready`), indicating a boundary that is orchestrationally coupled rather than explicitly modeled.
detail.suggestion: Define a dedicated runtime contact port/interface (e.g., `ContactRuntimePort`) implemented by `PlatformRuntime`, and make `ContactEventHandler` depend on that explicit port instead of multiple callbacks plus sink indirection.

review::.::holistic::test_strategy::call.summary: CALLBACK contract is only partially tested, missing non-async callback failure coverage

evidence: `ContactEventConfig.__post_init__` checks only that `on_event` is present for CALLBACK strategy, not that it is awaitable.
`ContactEventHandler._handle_callback` unconditionally does `await self._config.on_event(...)`, so a sync callback fails at runtime.
Current tests validate missing callback raises and async callback works, but do not include sync/non-awaitable callback cases.
detail.suggestion: Add explicit async-contract validation (for example, reject non-coroutine callbacks in `ContactEventConfig`) and add tests for sync callback rejection plus runtime behavior when invalid callbacks slip through.

review::.::holistic::test_strategy::hand.summary: Handler result API allows invalid statuses and tests miss malformed-status behavior

evidence: `HandlerResult.status` is type-hinted as `Literal[...]`, but dataclass construction does not enforce this at runtime.
`execute_dispatch_targets` handles only `ignored` and `error`; any other status falls through as success.
Router tests verify `handled`, `ignored`, and `error` outcomes but do not test malformed statuses from custom handlers.
detail.suggestion: Enforce runtime status validity (Enum or `__post_init__` guard) and add negative tests proving malformed statuses are rejected or mapped to explicit failures instead of implicit success.

review::.::holistic::test_strategy::send.summary: High-impact sender-type normalization helper lacks direct unit tests

evidence: `is_agent_sender_type` in `runtime/types.py` controls case-insensitive agent detection logic.
This helper is consumed in message-to-role conversion paths (`runtime/formatters.py`) and execution flow, but no direct test references `is_agent_sender_type`.
`tests/runtime/test_types.py` currently exercises `normalize_handle` and `PlatformMessage.format_for_llm`, not sender-type normalization edge cases.
detail.suggestion: Add parameterized tests for sender type normalization (`None`, whitespace, mixed case, unknown types) and one behavior-level assertion in formatting/execution tests to lock downstream role-mapping behavior.

review::.::holistic::test_strategy::non_.summary: Quality-gate governance is documented but not enforced as a single executable contract.

evidence: README.md states CI-required contract checks (bridge routing, bootstrap contracts, prompt contracts, integration marker policy).
pyproject.toml defines pytest defaults/markers, but there is no contract declaration that asserts README governance claims remain aligned with test configuration.
tests/support/integration/config.py repeats integration execution semantics in docstrings and policy code, creating multiple drift points for governance behavior.
detail.suggestion: Introduce one governance contract test module that parses/validates the README quality-gate section against canonical constants from test configuration (marker policy, integration command shape, required contract suites), and source README snippets from those constants where possible.

review::.::holistic::test_strategy::frag.summary: Integration-mode detection relies on substring matching of `-m` expression text.

evidence: In `_is_integration_mode`, integration detection is based on text checks (`"notintegration"` and `"integration"` substring membership after whitespace removal) rather than marker-token semantics.
pyproject.toml default `addopts = "-v -m 'not integration'"` makes this parser path central to policy behavior; expression variants can be misclassified by substring heuristics.
detail.suggestion: Replace substring logic with marker-aware evaluation (parse mark expression tokens or derive integration mode from collected test items/markers), and add targeted tests for mixed expressions to prevent false positives/negatives.

review::.::holistic::test_strategy::inco.summary: Test event factories use inconsistent default timestamp baselines across event types.

evidence: `make_message_event`/room helpers default to `2024-01-01...` while contact-related helpers default to `2026-01-01...`.
README.md positions contract checks as PR quality gates, but this shared fixture module introduces avoidable chronology skew that can mask ordering assumptions in cross-event tests.
detail.suggestion: Standardize all default fixture timestamps to one canonical baseline constant (or require explicit timestamps in ordering-sensitive tests) and add a small contract test asserting factory defaults remain coherent.

review::.::holistic::design_coherence::r.summary: Runner entrypoints duplicate orchestration flow, causing convention and behavior drift risk

evidence: All three runner files perform the same high-level sequence (read config/env, compose prompt, create adapter, create agent, run lifecycle), but each maintains its own env contract and defaults.
docker/claude_sdk/runner.py and examples/claude_sdk_docker/runner.py are especially close structurally yet diverge in required mounts and role/workspace handling.
Shared helper functions exist in runner_core, but orchestration decisions remain hand-assembled per file, so changes to startup policy require synchronized edits across entrypoints.
detail.suggestion: Introduce a declarative `RunnerSpec` + single generic runner executor in `src/thenvoi/testing/runner_core.py` (adapter factory, env schema, mount policy, optional repo init hooks), and keep each entrypoint as thin configuration-only wiring.

review::.::holistic::design_coherence::a.summary: Adapter modules repeat the same protocol/config scaffolding instead of a shared adapter pattern

evidence: Multiple adapters define near-identical `*ToolsProtocol` classes that primarily restate messaging + tool dispatch requirements.
Multiple adapters include a `from_config` classmethod that is largely parameter forwarding to `__init__`, repeating boilerplate mapping logic per adapter.
The repeated structural template suggests one intended adapter lifecycle pattern, but the pattern is implemented manually in each module.
detail.suggestion: Create a shared adapter construction scaffold (e.g., typed config protocol + reusable config-to-init utility and common tool-contract aliases) so each adapter only defines framework-specific behavior rather than repeating setup shape.

review::.::holistic::design_coherence::p.summary: Framework-specific tool wrappers duplicate behavior already centralized in tool_bridge

evidence: tool_bridge already normalizes args, dispatches tool calls, and builds consistent failure contracts.

claude_sdk/tools.py and parlant/tools.py still build separate per-tool wrapper functions (`_build_send_message_tool`, `_build_send_event_tool`, `_build_add_participant_tool`, etc.) with framework-local parsing/result shaping and error branches.
When platform tool semantics change, updates are needed in centralized bridge logic and multiple wrapper layers, increasing drift probability.
detail.suggestion: Define a single declarative tool descriptor table (name, arg schema adapter, response formatter) and generate framework-specific wrappers from that table while keeping dispatch and error semantics exclusively in `tool_bridge`.

review::.::holistic::design_coherence::g.summary: AgentTools centralizes too many unrelated responsibilities behind one broad protocol

evidence: `AgentToolsProtocol` combines messaging, participants, contacts, memory, schema export, and generic dispatch into one interface.
`AgentTools` implements all of these concerns in one class with many public async methods and internal helper responsibilities.
`FakeAgentTools` mirrors the same broad surface, forcing tests to carry large fake behavior even when only a narrow capability subset is required.
detail.suggestion: Split tool capabilities into composable service objects (messaging/participants/contacts/memory/schema-dispatch) and provide a thin aggregator only where required; migrate adapters to depend on the narrowest protocol slice they actually use.

review::.::holistic::cross_module_archit.summary: Example runtime bootstrap crosses into the testing package, blurring production-vs-test module boundaries.

evidence: `bootstrap.py` imports `resolve_agent_credentials` and `resolve_platform_urls` from `thenvoi.testing.config_loader` (`examples/common/bootstrap.py:11`).
Example scripts consume `bootstrap_agent()` as their runtime entrypoint (`examples/a2a_bridge/02_with_auth.py:25,58`), so examples inherit testing-layer dependency direction.

`thenvoi.testing.config_loader` includes compatibility aliases and runner-specific loading concerns (`load_runner_config`, `load_config`) that are not example-runtime specific (`src/thenvoi/testing/config_loader.py:62-82`).
detail.suggestion: Extract environment/credential resolution into a neutral runtime config module under non-testing package boundaries (for example `thenvoi.config`), then have `thenvoi.testing.config_loader` and `examples/common/bootstrap.py` both depend on that shared core.

review::.::holistic::cross_module_archit.summary: Bridge/platform tests maintain parallel ThenvoiLink mock surfaces instead of one shared contract boundary.

evidence: `tests/support/fakes_link.py` defines a canonical `make_thenvoi_link_mock()` with lifecycle/subscription/rest behavior (`24-89`).
`tests/bridge/conftest.py` rebuilds separate MagicMock link objects manually with partially overlapping methods (`23-58`) instead of reusing the shared fake.
`tests/platform/test_link_contacts.py` bypasses the shared fake and patches `WebSocketClient` directly in each test (`31-231`), creating a third pattern for the same boundary.
detail.suggestion: Define a single shared test-double boundary for `ThenvoiLink`/socket behavior (fixture factory + typed helper API), and migrate bridge/platform tests to consume it so contract changes are centralized.

review::.::holistic::cross_module_archit.summary: Integration support module is a hub that mixes plugin policy, live cleanup orchestration, settings cache, and fixtures, increasing cross-module coupling.

evidence: `tests/support/integration/config.py` contains pytest plugin hooks (`pytest_addoption`, `pytest_runtest_setup`) and fixture implementations in the same module (`87-190`, `540-711`).
The same module also owns a module-global mutable settings singleton (`_test_settings`) used across fixtures (`489-497`, `540-600`), introducing shared state coupling.

Integration tests consume marker/decorator layer (`tests/support/integration/markers.py`, `tests/integration/test_smoke.py:8-14`) while fixture enforcement policy lives elsewhere, splitting policy/runtime concerns across tightly coupled support modules.
detail.suggestion: Split integration architecture into explicit boundaries: a dedicated pytest plugin module for marker policy and command-line options, separate stateless fixture modules for clients/resources, and fixture-scoped settings objects instead of module-global cache.

review::.::holistic::abstraction_fitness.summary: Adapters use multiple incompatible tool-dispatch abstractions, duplicating glue logic and weakening the common tools interface.

evidence: `AnthropicAdapter` executes platform tools via `tools.execute_tool_call(tool_name, tool_input)`.
`PydanticAIAdapter` and `CrewAIAdapter` call `invoke_platform_tool(...)` directly and build framework-specific wrappers/signatures via `TOOL_BINDINGS`.
`ClaudeSDKAdapter` also routes through `invoke_platform_tool(...)` inside MCP handlers, bypassing the `ToolDispatchProtocol` path.
`AgentTools.execute_tool_call` still performs its own validation/dispatch path, so tool call policy and error-shaping are spread across adapter-local code and runtime tools.
detail.suggestion: Standardize on one dispatch contract. Prefer a single runtime-level `execute_tool_call` API with shared validation/error mapping and have all adapters call that surface (including MCP/SDK integrations), while `TOOL_BINDINGS` only describes schema shape.

review::.::holistic::abstraction_fitness.summary: Per-room/session state handling is reimplemented across adapters instead of being centralized, increasing lifecycle drift risk.

evidence: SimpleAdapter provides `stage_room_state`, `stage_room_history`, and cleanup helpers, but adapters still manage multiple custom room registries (`_message_history`, `_room_tools`, `_session_context`, `_session_ids`, `_room_sessions`, `_room_customers`, `_bootstrapped_rooms`).
Claude SDK stores multiple correlated dictionaries keyed by room (`_room_tools`, `_session_context`, `_session_ids`) with bespoke init/cleanup behavior.
Parlant tracks both session and customer mappings per room with adapter-specific lifecycle logic.
LangGraph carries separate bootstrap tracking set to prevent duplicate prompt injection, another room-lifecycle variant.
detail.suggestion: Introduce a shared room-session state manager abstraction (typed state container + bootstrap/cleanup policy hooks) and migrate adapters to register state schemas instead of each adapter hand-rolling room dictionaries.

review::.::holistic::abstraction_fitness.summary: The adapter `on_message` signature acts as a parameter bag, forcing all adapters to accept cross-cutting fields even when not relevant.

evidence: `SimpleAdapter.on_message` requires seven message-context arguments/flags (`msg`, `tools`, `history`, `participants_msg`, `contacts_msg`, `is_session_bootstrap`, `room_id`).
A2A gateway adapter receives this full signature but ignores participant/contact broadcast fields in its control-plane flow.
Tests construct full `AgentInput` payloads with many optional cross-cutting fields, confirming a broad generalized contract even for simpler handlers.
detail.suggestion: Replace the multi-parameter callback with a typed context object (for example `MessageTurnContext`) exposing optional facets, and split separate adapter base contracts for room-bound chat adapters vs control-plane adapters.

review::.::holistic::abstraction_fitness.summary: Compatibility wrappers remain in active runtime/test paths, adding pass-through indirection without clear enforcement of sunset.

evidence: A large forwarding registry in `compat/shims.py` still maps old module symbols (including runtime contact and bridge surfaces) through dynamic `__getattr__` forwarding.
`PlatformRuntime` keeps private alias wrappers that only forward (`_queue_broadcast`, `_inject_hub_event`, `_inject_hub_system_prompt`) alongside canonical methods.
Tests still import deprecated path `thenvoi.runtime.contact_tools`, demonstrating compatibility indirection remains a first-class dependency, not just legacy edge support.
detail.suggestion: Create and execute a compatibility retirement plan: track shim call sites, migrate tests/importers to canonical modules, add CI guardrails for new deprecated-path imports, and remove passthrough aliases once coverage confirms no active callers.

review::.::holistic::test_strategy::a2a_.summary: Bridge handler contract is tested mostly via router flow, leaving core API invariants under-specified at unit level.

evidence: `handler.py` exposes `HandlerResult` as a standalone API with validation and convenience constructors, but tests are embedded in router-focused scenarios rather than dedicated contract tests.
`tests/bridge/test_router.py` exercises routing and lifecycle marking heavily; it does not comprehensively assert `HandlerResult` constructor/factory defaults as a stable API surface.
This creates coupling where handler contract regressions can be masked by unrelated router behavior changes.
detail.suggestion: Add a focused test module (for example `tests/bridge/test_handler_contract.py`) that directly validates `HandlerResult` statuses, default/detail behavior, and constructor/factory parity independent of routing side effects.

review::.::holistic::test_strategy::a2a_.summary: Backward-compatible `None` return semantics declared in handler contract are not explicitly tested, despite being a critical dispatch compatibility path.

evidence: `BaseHandler.handle` return type in `handler.py` allows `HandlerResult | None` and documents `None` as backward-compatible implicit success.
`route_dispatch.py` contains explicit normalization logic for this (`if result is None: return HandlerResult.handled()` and fallback detail `legacy_implicit_return`).
Current bridge tests do not assert the `None`-return normalization behavior or the `legacy_implicit_return` compatibility branch.
detail.suggestion: Add an explicit test with a handler returning `None` and assert dispatch marks processed (not failed), plus a test for non-`HandlerResult` legacy return normalization to prevent silent compatibility regressions.

review::.::holistic::cross_module_archit.summary: Example runtime bootstrap depends on `thenvoi.testing` internals instead of a stable config boundary.

evidence: `examples/common/bootstrap.py` imports `resolve_agent_credentials` and `resolve_platform_urls` from `thenvoi.testing.config_loader` for normal example startup.
`README.md` positions examples as standard usage entrypoints, but this path relies on a namespace semantically tied to testing support.
No explicit compatibility contract is declared in `pyproject.toml` for `thenvoi.testing` as a public/stable example-facing API surface.
detail.suggestion: Promote credential/url resolution to a non-testing public module (for example `thenvoi.config`), update examples to consume that API, and keep `thenvoi.testing.config_loader` as a deprecation shim with a documented sunset.

review::.::holistic::test_strategy::qual.summary: Documented CI contract gates are not represented as enforceable repository-level test selection rules.

evidence: `README.md` claims PR-required contract checks (bridge/bootstrap/prompt/integration marker policy).
`pyproject.toml` only sets default pytest `addopts = "-v -m 'not integration'"` and marker registration; it does not encode a contract-check test suite selector or policy gate.
Given this split, governance depends on external CI wiring rather than an in-repo executable contract, increasing drift risk between docs and runtime checks.
detail.suggestion: Add explicit pytest marker groups or dedicated commands in `pyproject.toml` (or documented task runner targets) for each required contract gate, and reference those exact commands in `README.md` so policy is reproducible locally and in CI.

review::.::holistic::test_strategy::inte.summary: Integration fixture docs include commands that conflict with default pytest marker filtering.

evidence: `tests/support/integration/config.py` docstring repeatedly suggests `uv run pytest tests/integration/ -v` without `-m integration`.
`pyproject.toml` globally applies `-m 'not integration'`, so that command path can exclude integration tests by default.
`README.md` uses `-m integration` in integration sections, creating two competing run contracts inside the same codebase.
detail.suggestion: Normalize all integration run instructions to include `-m integration` (or update `addopts` strategy), and add a single authoritative command reference reused by both README and integration support module docs.

review::.::holistic::design_coherence::e.summary: ExecutionContext conflates multiple runtime responsibilities and duplicates message lifecycle logic.

evidence: `_process_backlog_message` and `_process_event` both implement near-identical lifecycle steps (mark_processing -> hydrate -> execute -> mark_processed/mark_failed), with separate branching and error paths.
`ExecutionContext` also owns participant cache mutation, context hydration, dedupe/retry tracking, stale message recovery, and queue synchronization in the same class, increasing change blast radius.
`PlatformRuntime` directly injects broadcast/system events into execution internals, reinforcing tight coupling to one oversized execution implementation.
detail.suggestion: Extract a shared `MessageLifecycleProcessor` used by both backlog and websocket paths, and split `ExecutionContext` into focused collaborators (sync/recovery, participant/context cache, queue executor). Keep `PlatformRuntime` talking to a narrow execution interface instead of execution internals.

review::.::holistic::design_coherence::a.summary: Large adapters bundle unrelated concerns (transport, policy, UX commands, and event formatting).

evidence: `CodexAdapter` contains local slash-command UX (`_handle_local_command`), manual approval queueing (`_resolve_manual_approval`), sandbox normalization, model fallback, RPC request handling, and event formatting in one class.
`CrewAIAdapter` combines global event-loop patching (`nest_asyncio`), dynamic tool class generation, execution-reporting policy, and chat history/session orchestration in one adapter module.
`ClaudeSDKAdapter` builds MCP server/tool wrappers inline (`_create_mcp_server`) while also handling session management and message processing.
detail.suggestion: Refactor adapters into composition-based components: session/transport client, turn/event processor, approval-policy service, and tool-binding factory. Keep adapter classes as thin framework facades wiring these components together.

review::.::holistic::design_coherence::t.summary: Framework-specific tool wrappers re-implement bridge/session behavior, causing design drift.

evidence: `tool_bridge.py` centralizes normalization/dispatch/error contracts, but `ClaudeSDKAdapter._create_mcp_server` independently defines result/error formatting and room-tool lookup closures.
`integrations/claude_sdk/tools.py` duplicates similar MCP wrapper concerns (`_make_result`, `_make_error`, `_get_tools`) and custom descriptor mapping logic.
`integrations/parlant/tools.py` uses module-level session registries (`_session_tools`, `_session_message_sent`) and custom formatting flows, creating another tool-execution path shape.
detail.suggestion: Introduce a shared framework-agnostic `ToolBindingFactory` + `ToolSessionRegistry` abstraction and make Claude/Parlant wrappers consume it. Keep only framework-specific decorator syntax in integration modules.

review::.::holistic::design_coherence::a.summary: Numbered gateway examples are thin wrappers over separate implementation files, inviting drift.

evidence: `02_with_demo_agent.py` contains long usage/docs but delegates execution to `with_demo_agent.main`, while the real runtime logic lives elsewhere.
`01_basic_gateway.py` follows the same wrapper pattern (delegating to another module), so explanatory docs and executable behavior can diverge over time.
This creates duplicate entrypoint conventions (`NN_*.py` wrappers + non-numbered implementations) for one example family.
detail.suggestion: Use a single canonical executable file per example and keep numbered variants as minimal aliases generated from that source (or remove aliases). Store documentation in one shared location to avoid wrapper-doc drift.

review::.::holistic::abstraction_fitness.summary: Adapters preserve two `on_message` contracts at once, forcing repetitive normalization/unpacking and weakening a single honest interface.

evidence: `SimpleAdapter.normalize_chat_turn` explicitly supports both context-style and legacy arg-bag invocation.
Each adapter `on_message` starts with `turn = self.normalize_chat_turn(...)` then repeats `msg = turn.msg`, `tools = turn.tools`, `history = turn.history` unpack boilerplate.
The same compatibility shape appears across framework adapters rather than being isolated to a migration boundary.
detail.suggestion: Move to a single `ChatMessageTurnContext`/`ControlMessageTurnContext` entrypoint, keep legacy support in one compatibility shim/decorator with a time-boxed deprecation plan, and remove per-adapter unpack scaffolding after callers/tests are migrated.

review::.::holistic::abstraction_fitness.summary: Tool protocol hierarchy is over-segmented relative to observed usage, increasing type indirection and maintenance without clear extension payoff.

evidence: `core/protocols.py` defines multiple layered protocols (`MessagingToolsProtocol`, `ParticipantToolsProtocol`, `ChatToolsProtocol`, `ContactToolsProtocol`, `MemoryToolsProtocol`, `PlatformToolOperationsProtocol`, `ToolSchemaProviderProtocol`, `ToolDispatchProtocol`, and composed variants).
`tests/core/test_protocols.py` uses one `_DummyTools` implementation and asserts conformance to almost all protocol layers, suggesting weak independent boundaries.

Adapters typically type against broad composed protocols anyway (`MessagingDispatchToolsProtocol`, `AnthropicSchemaToolsProtocol`), so many intermediary layers add conceptual hops more than isolation.
detail.suggestion: Collapse protocol layers to a smaller set aligned to real adapter seams (e.g., dispatch-only, dispatch+schema, full tools), and keep narrower interfaces only where a distinct implementation/caller pair exists.

review::.::holistic::abstraction_fitness.note: not reported in latest holistic re-import

review::.::holistic::abstraction_fitness.summary: A2A gateway exposes multiple peer state representations, and tests depend on private map mutation, indicating abstraction leakage and dual source-of-truth risk.

evidence: Adapter stores peer data in `_peer_directory` and also mirrors to `_peers` and `_peers_by_uuid`.
State is reassigned from directory (`self._peers = self._peer_directory.peers`, `self._peers_by_uuid = self._peer_directory.peers_by_uuid`) but tests directly set `_peers_by_uuid` private state.
Direct private-map coupling in tests shows callers are forced to know storage details instead of using a stable query API.
detail.suggestion: Make `PeerDirectory` the sole peer store, expose explicit read methods for test/runtime use, and remove mutable mirror maps (or make them read-only views) so consumers cannot bypass the canonical directory.

review::.::holistic::abstraction_fitness.summary: Example bootstrap/runner helpers form a broad wrapper surface that mostly forwards configuration to `Agent.create`, creating indirection without strong domain policy.

evidence: `bootstrap.py` chains thin wrappers (`load_runtime_config` -> `create_agent_from_runtime` -> `bootstrap_agent`) around URL/credential resolution and `Agent.create`.
`example_runners.py` repeats near-identical adapter construction and `Agent.create` orchestration across many `run_*_agent` functions with minor parameter variation.
Most wrappers are pass-through composition points rather than bounded behavior units, increasing maintenance touchpoints when runtime creation contracts change.
detail.suggestion: Introduce a small declarative runner spec (adapter factory + per-framework option transform) and one generic launch path; keep only truly framework-specific pre/post hooks as dedicated functions.

review::.::holistic::abstraction_fitness.note: not reported in latest holistic re-import

review::.::holistic::test_strategy::cont.summary: Quality-gate claims are broader than enforceable contracts visible in repo governance config

evidence: README.md declares required contract checks for bootstrap contracts and prompt contracts.
pyproject.toml defines a `contract_gate` marker string but no explicit governance map tying those claims to concrete target files/contracts.

High-importer contract-sensitive files (`examples/common/bootstrap.py`, `examples/prompts/characters.py`) do not expose a versioned contract artifact or manifest in scope that CI can verify for completeness.
detail.suggestion: Add a contract manifest (for example `tests/contracts/manifest.yaml`) that explicitly maps each claimed contract area to concrete test modules and covered files, and add a CI check that fails if README quality-gate claims and manifest entries drift.

review::.::holistic::test_strategy::cont.note: not reported in latest holistic re-import

review::.::holistic::test_strategy::inte.summary: Integration cleanup strategy can hide real failures and increase flaky outcomes

evidence: cleanup_contact_state in `tests/support/integration/config.py` records recoverable failures and, when best-effort is enabled, logs a warning then returns instead of failing the test run.
The same fixture layer uses bounded polling (`settle_timeout_s=5.0`) and repeated sleep-based retries for eventual consistency, which is environment-sensitive.
README.md presents this cleanup as a stability policy, but current behavior allows partial cleanup failure to pass, reducing confidence in isolation guarantees.
detail.suggestion: Split cleanup outcomes into strict vs advisory modes at marker level: keep best-effort only for explicitly labeled stress tests, and make default integration paths fail on any cleanup failure with structured diagnostics for each failed step.

review::.::holistic::test_strategy::inte.note: not reported in latest holistic re-import

review::.::holistic::test_strategy::mark.summary: Integration marker policy depends on duplicated declarations with no sync guard

evidence: `tests/support/integration/markers.py` composes runtime skip decorators from `INTEGRATION_MARKER_SPECS` and specific env-var assumptions (including first-item indexing for single-env markers).
`pyproject.toml` separately declares marker taxonomy (`requires_api`, `requires_multi_agent`, `requires_user_api`, `contract_gate`).
README.md claims integration marker policy is part of required quality gates, but no in-scope guard proves marker declarations and runtime skip policy remain synchronized.
detail.suggestion: Add a contract-gate test that imports marker decorators and asserts one-to-one parity with `pyproject.toml` marker definitions plus expected env requirements, so marker naming or requirement drift fails CI immediately.

review::.::holistic::test_strategy::mark.note: not reported in latest holistic re-import

review::.::holistic::design_coherence::a.summary: Legacy call-shape normalization is duplicated across adapters instead of being fully owned by base dispatch.

evidence: `SimpleAdapter.normalize_chat_turn` is defined as a compatibility normalization point, but adapter `chat(...)` methods still expose broad legacy params and manually unpack the normalized turn.

The same normalize-then-reassign structure appears in multiple adapters (`anthropic.py:140`, `claude_sdk.py:351`, `crewai.py:446`, `pydantic_ai.py:218`), indicating migration concerns are spread into provider implementations.
detail.suggestion: Move legacy signature support into a single base-level `chat` entrypoint (or decorator) that always passes a canonical `ChatTurn` into provider-specific methods; simplify adapter signatures to provider concerns only.

review::.::holistic::design_coherence::t.summary: Tool-call/tool-result reporting policy is reimplemented per adapter, causing repeated structure and behavior drift risk.

evidence: Each adapter/tooling module contains its own logic for emitting `tool_call` and `tool_result` events and handling reporting failures.

Repeated event-shaping and best-effort error handling patterns appear across modules (`anthropic.py:418+`, `pydantic_ai.py:292+`, `langgraph.py:266+`, `codex.py:896+`, `crewai_tooling.py:80+`, `claude_sdk.py:525+`).
detail.suggestion: Extract a shared runtime reporting component (input: tool name/args/result/error, execution context) and invoke it from adapters; keep adapters focused on framework integration points.

review::.::holistic::design_coherence::t.note: not reported in latest holistic re-import

review::.::holistic::design_coherence::t.summary: Claude and Parlant integrations maintain parallel tool catalogs and formatting rules rather than one canonical contract source.

evidence: Both modules define overlapping tool surfaces (`thenvoi_send_message`, `thenvoi_send_event`, participant/contact tools) and adapter-specific mapping logic.
The duplicated contract appears as independent schema/description/mapping implementations (`claude_sdk/tools.py:214+`, `parlant/tools.py:183+`, `parlant/tools.py:423+`).
detail.suggestion: Introduce a framework-agnostic canonical tool descriptor model in runtime, then generate Claude/Parlant-specific shapes from that model via thin translators.

review::.::holistic::design_coherence::t.note: not reported in latest holistic re-import

review::.::holistic::design_coherence::t.summary: Tool argument defaults and tool ordering are duplicated between runtime modules, creating synchronization debt.

evidence: `tool_bridge.py` maintains `PLATFORM_BASE_TOOL_ORDER`/`PLATFORM_MEMORY_TOOL_ORDER` while `tools.py` already defines `TOOL_MODELS` and derived tool-name sets; runtime assertions enforce sync after duplication (`tool_bridge.py:45-48`).
`normalize_tool_arguments` and `dispatch_platform_tool_call` both apply tool-specific default/coercion behavior in separate branches (`tool_bridge.py:167+` and `tool_bridge.py:242+`).
detail.suggestion: Derive order and argument contracts from one canonical registry (e.g., metadata attached to `TOOL_MODELS`) and have dispatch consume normalized arguments from a single normalization pass.

review::.::holistic::design_coherence::t.note: not reported in latest holistic re-import

review::.::holistic::design_coherence::a.summary: A2A gateway components keep mirrored peer caches alongside `PeerDirectory`, creating split state ownership.

evidence: Adapter keeps `_peers` and `_peers_by_uuid` dicts plus `_peer_directory`, then reassigns mirror fields from directory state (`adapter.py:104-106`, `adapter.py:151-153`).
Server similarly manages peer-directory responsibilities, increasing the chance of divergence across gateway-side peer lifecycle paths.
detail.suggestion: Make `PeerDirectory` the sole mutable store and expose read-only projections where required; remove mirrored caches and route lookups through directory APIs.

review::.::holistic::design_coherence::e.summary: Runner orchestration logic is duplicated across example and docker entrypoints, with behavioral drift between sibling runners.

evidence: `examples/run_agent.py` defines its own `RunnerSpec` and runner-selection orchestration while `runner_core.py` already provides shared runner contracts and execution primitives.

Claude docker runner and example docker runner are near-parallel entrypoints, but only docker variant performs repo initialization/context bundle injection (`docker/claude_sdk/runner.py:76`, `:102`) while example variant omits it.
detail.suggestion: Consolidate runner wiring through `runner_core` + shared composable hooks; keep each entrypoint as minimal configuration, and encode optional repo-context behavior as explicit feature flags.

review::.::holistic::design_coherence::e.note: not reported in latest holistic re-import

review::.::holistic::design_coherence::i.summary: Integration tests duplicate dynamic-agent lifecycle scaffolding and multi-step setup flows instead of reusing shared fixtures.

evidence: Both `test_dynamic_agent.py` and `test_participant_permissions.py` define their own `DynamicAgent` data structures and custom lifecycle management setup.
`test_hub_room_strategy.py` repeats approve/reject orchestration blocks with mostly identical setup and only outcome branching.
detail.suggestion: Extract a common dynamic-agent fixture/factory module for integration tests and parameterize approval/rejection scenario variants on top of shared setup helpers.

review::.::holistic::design_coherence::i.note: not reported in latest holistic re-import

review::.::holistic::design_coherence::n.summary: Runtime components use inconsistent nonfatal error recording patterns, fragmenting operational behavior.

evidence: `CodexWebSocketClient` uses `NonFatalErrorRecorder`, while `ThenvoiLink` and `RoomPresence` implement local `_nonfatal_errors` and bespoke `_record_nonfatal_error` methods.
Sibling codex transports differ in error-recording structure (`websocket_client.py` recorder-based vs `stdio_client.py` ad-hoc handling), indicating boundary inconsistency within one subsystem.
detail.suggestion: Standardize on one runtime nonfatal recorder abstraction (mixin or composed helper) and apply it consistently across link/presence/transports with unified metadata shape.

review::.::holistic::design_coherence::n.note: not reported in latest holistic re-import

review::.::holistic::cross_module_archit.summary: Integration support has a hub module that centralizes unrelated concerns and amplifies cross-file coupling.

evidence: `tests/support/integration/config.py` contains environment/settings access, cleanup workflows, peer selection, and multiple fixtures in one file.
`tests/support/integration/markers.py` separately defines marker composition, but tests consume both modules together for one integration workflow.
Changes to integration gating or fixture behavior require touching shared support modules used across many tests, increasing ripple risk.
detail.suggestion: Split integration support into explicit boundaries (for example `clients.py`, `cleanup.py`, `selection.py`, `fixtures.py`, `markers.py`) and keep each boundary imported via public APIs only; keep test modules depending on a thin fixture façade rather than internal helpers.

review::.::holistic::cross_module_archit.summary: A support module depends on another support module’s private symbols, creating brittle boundary contracts.

evidence: `tests/support/integration/config.py` imports `tests.support.integration.plugin as integration_plugin` and aliases underscored functions (`_enforce_live_fixture_policy`, `_is_integration_mode`, `_is_truthy_env`).
These names are explicitly private by convention, so cross-module reuse can break on internal refactors without clear contract signaling.
detail.suggestion: Expose a small public policy interface from the plugin module (non-underscored functions or a dataclass/service object) and update config/fixture modules to consume only that public surface.

review::.::holistic::high_level_elegance.summary: Integration clients reimplement similar lifecycle orchestration patterns instead of sharing one architectural runtime primitive.

evidence: `ClaudeSessionManager` uses command queues plus background task lifecycle (`start`, `stop`, `_run_session_loop`, per-command futures).
`CodexStdioClient` independently manages process creation, dual read loops, cancellation, and pending-request failure logic in `connect`/`close`.
Both modules solve similar ownership/shutdown concerns with different semantics, increasing long-term divergence and maintenance cost.
detail.suggestion: Introduce a shared async integration lifecycle base (task registry, cancellation policy, close semantics, pending-operation failure policy) and have both integration managers compose/extend it.

review::.::holistic::high_level_elegance.summary: Behavioral policy text is embedded directly in runtime/example code, blurring ownership between policy content and execution logic.

evidence: `HUB_ROOM_SYSTEM_PROMPT` in `formatting.py` is a large operational policy block colocated with event formatting functions.
`examples/prompts/characters.py` encodes large character-policy templates in module-level string constants.
Prompt/policy updates require code edits in logic modules rather than updating dedicated policy assets or a prompt registry.
detail.suggestion: Move prompt/policy bodies into a dedicated prompt-policy package or data assets with explicit versioned loading, and keep runtime modules focused on formatting/execution concerns.

review::.::holistic::convention_outlier:.summary: Example entrypoints mix package imports, bare local imports, and sys.path mutation.

evidence: examples/a2a_bridge/01_basic_agent.py imports `setup_logging` via `from setup_logging import setup_logging` instead of package-qualified import.
examples/a2a_gateway/01_basic_gateway.py imports `main` from `examples.a2a_gateway.basic_gateway` using package-qualified style.
tests/example_agents/conftest.py injects example directories into sys.path to make imports work.
detail.suggestion: Standardize example import conventions around package-qualified imports and run examples as modules (`python -m ...`/`uv run -m ...`); remove sys.path mutation by making examples importable through a stable package boundary.

review::.::holistic::convention_outlier:.summary: Runner startup logic is duplicated with divergent conventions across docker and examples.

evidence: docker/claude_sdk/runner.py and examples/claude_sdk_docker/runner.py both orchestrate bootstrap_runner, prompt composition, adapter creation, and run lifecycle with mostly parallel code paths.
The two files diverge on required mounts and prompt composition inputs (repo init/context only in docker variant), despite serving the same runner role.
docker/shared/config_loader.py is a pure pass-through alias to thenvoi.testing.config_loader.load_runner_config, adding another convention surface without behavior.
detail.suggestion: Extract a single shared runner composition function/module and keep environment-specific differences as explicit config data (mounts, repo-init flag, prompt extras), then collapse pass-through wrappers that add no policy.

review::.::holistic::error_consistency::.summary: Integration boundaries mix exception propagation, in-band error payloads, and silent fallback.

evidence: examples/a2a_gateway/basic_gateway.py catches Exception and re-raises ValueError for runtime loading failures.
examples/langgraph/standalone_sql_agent.py catches Exception from download and silently creates an unrelated fallback DB schema.
src/thenvoi/integrations/parlant/tools.py converts failures to `tool_result_type(data=...)` text errors rather than raising typed failures.
examples/langgraph/standalone_calculator.py reports errors via `{"result": None, "error": ...}` return shape instead of exception or typed result object.
detail.suggestion: Define one integration error contract (typed exception hierarchy or typed result object) and apply it uniformly at seams; only convert to human-readable strings at final UI/output boundaries.

review::.::holistic::mid_level_elegance:.summary: Module handoffs rely on ad-hoc seam contracts instead of one explicit boundary protocol.

evidence: docker/shared/config_loader.py is a pass-through seam with no translation/policy, while runners still own orchestration and validation details.
docker/claude_sdk/runner.py and examples/claude_sdk_docker/runner.py duplicate seam choreography with subtle differences, indicating missing shared boundary abstraction.
src/thenvoi/integrations/parlant/tools.py builds a separate ad-hoc error/result transport protocol (`tool_result_type(data=...)`) that is not shared by neighboring integrations.
detail.suggestion: Introduce a shared seam contract for runner/tool integrations (common bootstrap spec + typed boundary result/error model), then refactor entrypoints to compose that contract rather than re-implementing handoffs per module.

review::.::holistic::abstraction_fitness.summary: Legacy arg-bag compatibility keeps adapter boundary contracts wider than needed.

evidence: `SimpleAdapter` and `ControlPlaneAdapter` normalize legacy arg-bag calls into turn-context objects, but `CodexAdapter.on_message` still publicly exposes the full legacy argument bag in its signature and manually normalizes.
Assigned tests actively call adapters with legacy-style parameter bags (`msg/tools/history/...`) rather than a single typed turn context, showing the wide seam is still in active use.
detail.suggestion: Introduce one canonical `on_message(turn=...)` contract for new code and confine arg-bag compatibility to thin adapters generated by a shared compatibility layer; migrate tests to typed turn contexts first, then deprecate direct arg-bag signatures (starting with `CodexAdapter`).

review::.::holistic::abstraction_fitness.summary: Composite tool protocol is too broad for many adapter use cases.

evidence: `AgentToolsProtocol` combines chat, contacts, memory, schema export, and dispatch into one large surface.

Multiple adapters type against `AgentToolsProtocol` even when they primarily need message/event + tool dispatch behavior, while `LangGraphAdapter` demonstrates a narrower protocol (`MessagingDispatchToolsProtocol`) is viable.
Protocol runtime-check tests enforce full-surface implementations, increasing coupling and mock burden.
detail.suggestion: Split adapter dependencies to minimal capability protocols per integration path (for example: dispatch+messaging, contact-aware, memory-aware) and migrate adapters/tests to those slices; keep `AgentToolsProtocol` as a convenience aggregate, not the default dependency type.

review::.::holistic::dependency_health::.summary: Optional integration dependencies use conflicting import/failure strategies.

evidence: `crewai.py` imports optional packages in module scope and raises on import failure.
`langgraph.py` imports framework types at module import time without a deferred availability guard.
`claude_sdk.py` captures ImportError and defers hard failure through `_ensure_claude_sdk_available()`, creating a different operational contract from sibling adapters.
detail.suggestion: Standardize optional dependency policy across adapters: either uniformly defer failures to adapter construction/start with explicit guard helpers, or uniformly fail at import with documented behavior; implement one shared helper and apply it across all optional integrations.

review::.::holistic::mid_level_elegance:.summary: Tool-dispatch seam conflates failures with successful string payloads.

evidence: `AgentTools.execute_tool_call()` returns error text strings for validation and dispatch failures instead of a typed failure result or raised exception.
`CodexAdapter._handle_server_request()` treats any returned string as normal output and sends `success: true`; only exception paths produce `success: false`.
Current tests verify exception-driven validation failure handling, but not the returned-error-string path, leaving this contract mismatch exposed.
detail.suggestion: Replace string-sentinel failures with a typed result (for example `ToolExecutionResult{ok,error,payload}`) or raise explicit exceptions from `execute_tool_call`; update adapter bridge code to map failures to `success: false` consistently and add tests for unknown-tool/dispatch-failure paths.

review::.::holistic::mid_level_elegance:.summary: Session store leaks mutable internal state across module boundaries.

evidence: InMemorySessionStore.get() returns the stored SessionData object directly (`return self._sessions.get(room_id)`), and list_sessions() returns direct references (`list(self._sessions.values())`).
SessionData is a mutable Pydantic model; callers can mutate `last_activity` without going through store APIs, changing eviction behavior externally.
Runtime boundary types in runtime/types.py are also mutable dataclasses, reinforcing a pattern of unguarded cross-module state mutation.
detail.suggestion: Make boundary DTOs immutable (or return defensive copies) and provide explicit store methods for lifecycle updates (for example, `touch_session(room_id)`), so session mutation always flows through lock-protected store logic.

review::.::holistic::mid_level_elegance:.summary: Bridge handler contract mixes implicit and explicit outcome semantics.

evidence: BaseHandler.handle in handler.py returns `HandlerResult | None`, with docstring saying `None` means implicit success.
HandlerResult introduces explicit statuses (`handled`, `ignored`, `error`), but the same seam still permits unstructured completion via `None`.
Nearby runtime seam (`MessageHandler` in runtime/types.py) uses a different contract style (`Awaitable[None]`), increasing integration ambiguity between related handler abstractions.
detail.suggestion: Unify handler seams on one explicit contract: require structured outcome objects for bridge handlers (deprecate implicit `None`), and align runtime handler type aliases/docs so boundary behavior is uniformly predictable.

review::.::holistic::test_strategy::high.summary: Current boundary design creates silent-failure paths that need targeted contract tests.

evidence: handler.py intentionally allows implicit success via `None`, so accidental handler return-shape regressions can look successful unless tests explicitly assert contract normalization behavior.
session.py exposes live session objects, so behavior can be altered externally without store API calls, making tests vulnerable to hidden coupling and stateful side effects.
Both files represent critical routing/session paths, but their contracts rely on implicit behavior rather than fail-fast boundaries, increasing the chance of regressions escaping unit tests.
detail.suggestion: Add strict contract tests around these seams: enforce explicit handler outcomes (or explicit normalization warnings/errors), and add tests that verify session state cannot be mutated externally without going through store methods.

review::.::holistic::package_organizatio.summary: Runtime root mixes core API surface with legacy compatibility modules, weakening folder ownership boundaries.

evidence: `src/thenvoi/runtime/__init__.py` documents a core-only runtime surface and directs domain helpers to dedicated submodules.
`src/thenvoi/runtime/contact_tools.py` is explicitly a deprecated compatibility shim pointing elsewhere, yet remains at runtime root.
`src/thenvoi/runtime/custom_tools.py` remains in runtime root and is marked orphaned in holistic context, indicating lingering legacy placement.
detail.suggestion: Staged reorg plan: (1) Target folder: consolidate legacy shims under `src/thenvoi/runtime/compat/` (or `src/thenvoi/compat/runtime/`) and keep runtime root focused on core runtime contracts only. (2) Move order: first add new compat modules and wire shim registry; second update imports in code/tests from `thenvoi.runtime.contact_tools`/`thenvoi.runtime.custom_tools` to canonical modules (`thenvoi.runtime.contacts.contact_tools` and the chosen custom-tools home); third deprecate and remove root-level stragglers. (3) Import-update/validation commands: `rg -n "thenvoi\.runtime\.(contact_tools|custom_tools)" src tests`, then run `uv run pytest tests/runtime/test_contact_handler.py tests/runtime/test_contact_tools.py tests/runtime/test_custom_tools.py -v` and `uv run pytest tests/test_agent.py -v`.

review::.::holistic::package_organizatio.summary: Contacts-related tests are split between a flat runtime test directory and a dedicated contacts subpackage, reducing navigability and ownership clarity.

evidence: Assigned contacts tests live in `tests/runtime/` even though a dedicated `tests/runtime/contacts/` package exists.
holistic_context.structure reports `tests/runtime/` has 20 files (flat overload signal) while contacts-specific tests also exist in `tests/runtime/contacts/`.
Both assigned files import contacts-domain symbols (`thenvoi.runtime.contacts.*`), indicating affinity with the contacts test package.
detail.suggestion: Staged reorg plan: (1) Target folder: move `tests/runtime/test_contact_handler.py` and `tests/runtime/test_contact_tools.py` into `tests/runtime/contacts/` with names aligned to existing package conventions. (2) Move order: move one file at a time, keep temporary import compatibility where needed, then normalize local fixture imports in the moved tests. (3) Import-update/validation commands: `rg -n "tests\.runtime\.test_contact_(handler|tools)|runtime/test_contact_(handler|tools)" tests`, run `uv run pytest tests/runtime/contacts/ -k "contact_handler or contact_tools" -v`, then `uv run pytest tests/runtime/ -v`.

review::.::holistic::high_level_elegance.summary: The runtime package’s declared public contract is not consistently reinforced by nearby tests, leaving the intended architectural boundary implicit.

evidence: `src/thenvoi/runtime/__init__.py` defines a curated runtime surface via lazy exports.
`tests/test_agent.py` primarily imports concrete internals (`thenvoi.runtime.types`) rather than asserting boundary behavior through `thenvoi.runtime` surface.
`tests/conftest.py` wires broad shared plugins, but no package-boundary contract test is present among assigned files for runtime root exports/placement promises.
detail.suggestion: Staged reorg plan: (1) Target folders: keep contract tests in `tests/runtime/` and domain tests in `tests/runtime/contacts/`; add a dedicated runtime-surface contract test file under `tests/runtime/` to lock intended import boundary. (2) Move order: first add runtime-surface assertions (`thenvoi.runtime` exports and deprecation import paths), second relocate contacts behavior tests to contacts subfolder, third remove redundant direct-internal import assertions where boundary tests replace them. (3) Import-update/validation commands: `rg -n "from thenvoi\.runtime(\.| import)" tests`, then `uv run pytest tests/runtime/test_namespace.py tests/runtime/test_tools_direct_module.py tests/test_agent.py -v`.

review::.::holistic::cross_module_archit.summary: Integration config depends on private plugin symbols, creating fragile cross-module contracts.

evidence: `tests/support/integration/config.py` binds `_enforce_live_fixture_policy`, `_is_integration_mode`, and `_is_truthy_env` from `tests.support.integration.plugin` using private-name imports.
Private-name reach-in bypasses explicit module contracts and increases break risk when plugin internals change.
Marker and fixture policy logic are split across multiple modules, increasing boundary ambiguity.
detail.suggestion: Promote a public policy API in `tests/support/integration/plugin.py` (for example `integration_policy.py` exports), update `config.py` and `markers.py` to consume only public functions/constants, and add a small contract test asserting policy API behavior and import stability.

review::.::holistic::high_level_elegance.summary: README governance promises are not encoded in repository-local default execution paths.

evidence: `README.md` says contract checks are required on every PR and points to `uv run pytest -m contract_gate -v`.
`pyproject.toml` default pytest options are `-m 'not integration'` and do not include `contract_gate`, so default local test runs can miss declared required contracts.
Policy intent exists in docs and marker definitions, but execution defaults do not force/compose the policy.
detail.suggestion: Make governance executable: add a dedicated test alias/script (for example `test:quality-gates`) in project tooling, include `contract_gate` in that canonical command, and reference exactly one authoritative command in README and CI so docs/runtime policy cannot drift.

review::.::holistic::test_strategy::inte.summary: Integration test admission policy is duplicated between markers and fixture-time skips.

evidence: Marker decorators (`requires_api`, `requires_multi_agent`, `requires_user_api`) encode env requirements.
Fixtures in `config.py` independently call `pytest.skip(...)` when env vars are absent, allowing tests without required markers to still pass as skipped.
This creates two policy layers with different enforcement points, weakening detection of mis-marked tests.
detail.suggestion: Centralize admission in one layer: enforce marker presence for live fixtures (hard-fail on missing marker in integration mode), keep env-check logic in a shared policy function, and reserve fixture skips only for explicitly marked integration tests.

review::.::holistic::package_organizatio.summary: High-fan-in reusable modules are located in `examples/`, obscuring ownership boundaries.

evidence: `examples/common/bootstrap.py` and `examples/prompts/characters.py` are among top-imported files (16 and 13 importers respectively), used across multiple framework examples and tests.
Holistic directory profiles classify `examples/prompts/` as script zone while these files behave like shared contracts.
Current placement implies demo scope even though repo governance treats them as contract-check targets.
detail.suggestion: Staged reorg plan: 1) create `src/thenvoi/example_support/bootstrap.py` and `src/thenvoi/example_support/prompts/characters.py`; 2) move logic there while leaving thin compatibility shims in old `examples/` paths; 3) update imports in framework examples and tests to new package paths; 4) remove shims after one release cycle. Validate with `uv run ruff check . --fix`, `uv run pyrefly check`, `uv run pytest tests/ --ignore=tests/integration/ -v`, and `uv run pytest -m contract_gate -v`.

review::.::holistic::design_coherence::a.summary: Adapter API is split between legacy arg-bag and typed turn-context contracts.

evidence: `SimpleAdapter.normalize_chat_turn()` and `ControlPlaneAdapter.normalize_control_turn()` both accept either context objects or legacy parameter bags.
Most adapters use `@legacy_chat_turn_compat` with context-only signatures, but `CodexAdapter.on_message()` still directly exposes union/optional legacy parameters and performs manual normalization.
This keeps two call styles alive simultaneously and leaks compatibility mechanics into business handlers.
detail.suggestion: Define one canonical adapter entrypoint (`on_message(turn_context)`), move legacy compatibility into a temporary boundary wrapper outside adapter implementations, and enforce the canonical signature across all adapters.

review::.::holistic::design_coherence::r.summary: Runtime orchestration and tool execution are concentrated into oversized multi-role classes.

evidence: `ExecutionContext` (978 LOC) owns room state, queue/process loop, participant tracking, dedupe cache, retry strategy, sync reconciliation, and shutdown/idle signaling in one class.
`AgentTools` (1159 LOC) contains Pydantic input models, tool schema rendering for providers, business operations, and dispatch/error translation in one implementation.
Protocols are split into granular interfaces, but concrete runtime wiring collapses them back into broad single classes, reducing leverage of those boundaries.
detail.suggestion: Split runtime into focused components (e.g., message loop coordinator, participant state service, history hydration service, tool schema provider, tool dispatcher), then compose them behind thin facades.

review::.::holistic::design_coherence::t.summary: Tool argument/default logic is duplicated across bridge and runtime dispatch layers.

evidence: `tool_bridge.py` has both `normalize_tool_arguments()` and `dispatch_platform_tool_call()` with repeated per-tool defaulting/shape logic.
`AgentTools.execute_tool_call()` performs its own validation/normalization path before delegating to bridge dispatch, creating overlapping responsibility.
There is already visible duplication noise (`thenvoi_send_event` sets `metadata` default twice in `normalize_tool_arguments()`), indicating drift risk from parallel logic tables.
detail.suggestion: Create one authoritative tool contract registry (arguments, defaults, dispatcher binding, error formatting) and have `AgentTools`, bridge dispatch, and binding factories consume that registry instead of re-encoding behavior.

review::.::holistic::design_coherence::a.summary: Error event reporting pattern is copy-pasted across adapters instead of centralized.

evidence: Multiple adapters implement nearly identical `_report_error()` logic (`tools.send_event('Error: ...', 'error')` + broad exception fallback).
LangGraph repeats the same best-effort error-event block inline inside `on_message()` instead of using a shared helper.
This duplicates failure semantics and increases chance of subtle divergence in nonfatal handling.
detail.suggestion: Extract a shared adapter error-reporting utility/mixin that standardizes error event payloads and nonfatal recording, then remove per-adapter bespoke copies.

review::.::holistic::design_coherence::r.summary: Runner entrypoints reimplement the same bootstrap/prompt/agent lifecycle skeleton.

evidence: Each runner builds similar flow: bootstrap config, resolve role/prompt, instantiate adapter, create runner agent, log startup, run lifecycle.
Small variant differences are mixed into full copies of the same lifecycle logic, especially between Claude runner variants.
`examples/run_agent.py` carries another parallel runner-spec/kwargs composition path rather than reusing one runner composition abstraction.
detail.suggestion: Move to a single declarative runner template API in `runner_core` where adapter-specific options are provided as data/hooks, not copied `main()` flows.

review::.::holistic::design_coherence::i.summary: Large integration test files mix provisioning, orchestration, and assertion concerns.

evidence: `test_codex_adapter.py` concentrates fake transport implementation, schema helpers, setup utilities, and a very large behavior matrix in one module.
`test_participant_permissions.py` combines dynamic resource lifecycle management, multiple fixtures, and two broad matrix suites in a single file.
`test_multi_agent.py` and `test_history_converters.py` include heavy scenario setup plus assertions together, increasing cognitive load and reducing focused test intent.
detail.suggestion: Split scenario families into focused modules, move reusable provisioning/fake clients into shared test support packages, and isolate matrix generation from assertion logic.

review::.::holistic::ai_generated_debt::.summary: Claude SDK runner flow is copy-pasted across production and example runners with only small parameter differences.

evidence: Both runner files perform the same sequence: bootstrap config, derive model/prompt/tools/workspace/role, instantiate ClaudeSDKAdapter, create runner agent, log startup fields, and call run_agent_lifecycle().
Differences are mostly mount list and optional repo_init wiring, indicating duplication of orchestration template rather than distinct domain logic.
A compatibility wrapper (docker/shared/config_loader.py) also re-exports thin config-loading behavior, reinforcing shallow layering around duplicated runner scaffolding.
detail.suggestion: Extract one shared runner-template builder in thenvoi.testing.runner_core (or a dedicated runner module) that accepts a small dataclass of overrides (mounts, extra prompt sections, repo init hook), and keep docker/example entrypoints as minimal declarative specs.

review::.::holistic::ai_generated_debt::.summary: Several integration tests contain excessive narration and decorative logging that duplicates executable assertions.

evidence: Tests repeatedly emit separator banners and procedural logs ("Testing:", repeated agent identity logs, scenario narration) before/after assertions.
Large block comments and docstrings restate what the test method body already encodes step-by-step.
Signal-to-noise is reduced in failures because many log lines are descriptive prose rather than unique diagnostic context.
detail.suggestion: Adopt a lean integration-test logging convention: keep only failure-relevant IDs/state snapshots, remove banner prose, and move scenario explanations to short class/module docstrings or parametrized test names.

review::.::holistic::ai_generated_debt::.summary: Adapter/converter tests repeat long boilerplate preambles that provide little per-file specificity.

evidence: Each file starts with a similarly structured paragraph explaining that shared behavior lives elsewhere and this file contains framework-specific checks.
The repeated template text is long relative to unique content and changes rarely, creating synchronization drift risk across files.
The pattern suggests generated boilerplate propagation instead of a single canonical test documentation location.
detail.suggestion: Replace repeated per-file preambles with a short one-liner and centralize shared testing policy in one helper module or docs page referenced from each file.

review::.::holistic::convention_outlier:.summary: Sibling example entrypoints follow inconsistent logging/bootstrap/import conventions, creating style islands.

evidence: `examples/run_agent.py` and coding scripts configure logging directly with `logging.basicConfig`, while many examples use shared logging profiles.
`examples/codex/basic_agent.py` imports adapter classes from `thenvoi.adapters.codex`, while peer examples use `from thenvoi.adapters import ...`.
Bootstrap flow is inconsistent across example families (shared `examples.common.bootstrap` in some, bespoke setup in others).
detail.suggestion: Define and enforce one example-entrypoint contract: standardized logging helper, standardized adapter import path policy, and a shared bootstrap utility for credentials/platform URLs. Add a lightweight lint/test that validates example modules against this contract.

review::.::holistic::error_consistency::.summary: Equivalent runtime failures are handled with incompatible strategies (raise, return, stringify), making caller behavior unpredictable.

evidence: `ParlantAdapter.on_message` returns after logging when app is uninitialized instead of raising.
`LangGraphAdapter.on_message` emits an error event then re-raises exceptions.
`CrewAIToolRuntime.execute_tool` catches exceptions and returns JSON error payloads as normal string results.
`generate_token.py` mixes `SystemExit` and `ValueError` for related failure classes.
detail.suggestion: Create a shared error policy matrix by boundary (adapter internal, tool execution, script CLI) and implement common helpers: invariant failures should raise typed exceptions, user-visible failures should be emitted once with preserved context, and CLI scripts should normalize exit behavior through one exception-to-exit mapper.

review::.::holistic::abstraction_fitness.summary: Abstractions are uneven: oversized adapter classes coexist with low-value forwarding wrappers.

evidence: `src/thenvoi/adapters/codex.py` (1837 LOC) aggregates transport lifecycle, approval protocol, model fallback, tool dispatch, and event emission.
`docker/shared/config_loader.py` is a pass-through adapter to `thenvoi.testing.config_loader.load_runner_config` with no policy translation.
`crewai_tooling.py` and `pydantic_ai.py` both build dynamic tool wrappers with overlapping concerns instead of sharing a bounded abstraction.
detail.suggestion: Split `CodexAdapter` into focused collaborators (client lifecycle, approval manager, turn/event mapper, model policy). Remove/pass-inline no-policy wrappers like `docker/shared/config_loader.py`. Extract a common adapter-tool binding layer consumed by CrewAI and PydanticAI adapters.

review::.::holistic::ai_generated_debt::.summary: Example scenarios are manually replicated across frameworks with minimal semantic differences.

evidence: Each framework keeps separate basic/custom/Tom/Jerry scripts with highly similar orchestration and only adapter-level substitutions.
Prompt and roleplay behaviors are centralized, but surrounding script scaffolding remains duplicated.
This duplication pattern is repeated for multiple scenario families, increasing synchronized-edit burden.
detail.suggestion: Adopt a scenario registry (scenario definition + framework adapter factory) that generates or dispatches framework variants from shared scenario definitions. Keep framework-specific deltas in small adapter hooks rather than full script copies.

review::.::holistic::package_organizatio.summary: Directory layout shows objective overload in examples and adapters, reducing navigability and ownership clarity.

evidence: `examples/langgraph/` has `file_count: 12` (all script zone) and mixes tutorial examples with standalone app-like utilities.
`examples/` directory profile contains mixed `production` and `script` zones, signaling blurred placement policy.
`src/thenvoi/adapters/` has `file_count: 12`, `total_loc: 5322`, with one dominant file (`codex.py`) acting as a structural hotspot.
detail.suggestion: Staged reorg plan: Stage 1 target folders: move standalone utilities into `examples/langgraph/standalone/` and numbered walkthroughs into `examples/langgraph/scenarios/`; move cross-framework scenario prompts into `examples/scenarios/prompts/`. Stage 2 move order: (1) create new folders and move passive modules (`prompts.py`, standalone files), (2) move numbered examples, (3) update `examples/run_agent.py` registry paths. Stage 3 adapter packaging: split `src/thenvoi/adapters/codex.py` into `src/thenvoi/adapters/codex/{adapter.py,approval.py,turns.py,events.py}` and keep `__init__.py` as facade. Stage 4 import and validation commands: `uv run ruff check . --fix`, `uv run ruff format .`, `uv run pyrefly check`, `uv run pytest tests/adapters/test_codex_adapter.py tests/example_agents/ -v`.

review::.::holistic::design_coherence::a.summary: A shared adapter lifecycle exists conceptually but is implemented with diverging per-framework policies and duplicated orchestration code.

evidence: All adapters perform similar steps (bootstrap metadata/history, inject system updates, invoke framework/tool loop, report/send errors), but each file hand-rolls these seams differently.
Shared helper scope is narrow (`system_updates` only), while lifecycle/error/reporting composition is duplicated across adapters.
Behavioral divergence appears in invariant checks and failure propagation style, indicating pattern drift rather than intentional specialization only.
detail.suggestion: Introduce a stricter adapter skeleton in core (template method hooks for history staging, metadata injection, tool reporting, and error propagation). Keep framework-specific calls in narrow hook methods and enforce conformance with shared adapter contract tests.
